availableCommands.name: debug
availableCommands.description: Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues (bundled)

availableCommands.name: simplify
availableCommands.description: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found. (bundled)

availableCommands.name: batch
availableCommands.description: Research and plan a large-scale change, then execute it in parallel across 5–30 isolated worktree agents that each open a PR. (bundled)

availableCommands.name: loop
availableCommands.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) (bundled)

availableCommands.name: claude-api

Build apps with the Claude API or Anthropic SDK.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`/`claude_agent_sdk`, or user asks to use Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Agent SDK.
DO NOT TRIGGER when: code imports `openai`/other AI SDK, general programming, or ML/data-science tasks. (bundled)

availableCommands.name: create-workflow
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Formalize repeatable patterns into trackable kspec workflows. Design steps, choose triggers, create loop variants, and integrate with skills.

availableCommands.name: help
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Get help with kspec commands and workflows

availableCommands.name: observe
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Capture and act on systemic patterns — friction, successes, questions, and ideas. The feedback loop that drives process improvement.

availableCommands.name: plan
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Translate approved plans into specs and tasks. Import structured documents or create incrementally. Plans persist as durable artifacts with audit trail.

availableCommands.name: reflect
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Structured reflection at the end of work sessions. Identifies learnings, friction points, and improvements for system evolution.

availableCommands.name: review
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Kspec-specific review gates — spec alignment, own AC coverage, trait AC coverage, and validation integration. Building block for project-specific review workflows.

availableCommands.name: task-work
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Structured task lifecycle — start, work, note, submit, complete. Fix cycle handling, scope management, loop mode for automation, and quality gates.

availableCommands.name: triage
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Triage inbox items systematically. Records decisions with audit trail, then executes actions. Supports inbox, observations, and automation eligibility triage.

availableCommands.name: triage-automation
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Assess and prepare tasks for automation eligibility. Verify spec coverage, acceptance criteria, and task readiness for automated agents.

availableCommands.name: triage-inbox
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Process inbox items using the record-act pattern. Categorize, promote to spec/task, merge duplicates, defer, or delete stale items with full audit trail.

availableCommands.name: writing-specs
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Create and maintain specification items — modules, features, requirements, acceptance criteria, and traits. The source of truth for what to build.

availableCommands.name: compact
availableCommands.description: Clear conversation history but keep a summary in context. Optional: /compact [instructions for summarization]

availableCommands.description: Show current context usage

availableCommands.description: Dump the JS heap to ~/Desktop

────────────────────────────────────────
ULID:      01KKAWM15XTDNE9XPRPADT9R88

Status:    pending
Priority:  2

─── Description ───
  Investigate and fix bugs in connectivity layer. Check: orchestrator port fallback, rate limiter window reset, API prompt 4s delay race condition, pending prompt not consumed on spawn failure, mobile token regeneration during connections, WebSocket subscription leak, CORS config.
Spec ref:  @orchestrator
Created:   2026-03-10T03:28:32.445Z

─── Spec Context ───
MCP Orchestrator
Type: feature
Implementation: implemented
Description:
  Localhost HTTP API (port 19836) enabling MCP-based cross-session control. List sessions, read output (last N lines), send input to any PTY. Companion MCP server script for Claude CLI integration.
Acceptance Criteria:
  [ac-1]
    Given: multiple PTY sessions are running
    When: GET /sessions is called on localhost:19836
    Then: a JSON array of all sessions is returned with id, cwd, backend, projectPath, projectName, and spawnedAt
  [ac-2]
    Given: a PTY session exists
    When: POST /sessions/{id}/input is called with text
    Then: the text followed by carriage return is written to the PTY, simulating user keyboard input

1→/**
     2→ * Authentication Rate Limiting Module
     3→ *
     4→ * Provides rate limiting for failed authentication attempts.
     5→ */
     6→
     7→import type { RateLimitEntry } from './types.js'
     8→
     9→// Constants
    10→const RATE_LIMIT_MAX_ATTEMPTS = 5
    11→const RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS = 15 * 60 * 1000 // 15 minutes
    12→const RATE_LIMIT_BLOCK_DURATION_MS = 15 * 60 * 1000 // 15 minutes
    13→
    14→const rateLimitStore = new Map<string, RateLimitEntry>()
    15→
    16→/**
    17→ * Check if an IP is rate limited
    18→ * Returns { allowed: boolean, retryAfter?: number }
    19→ */
    20→export function checkRateLimit(ip: string): { allowed: boolean; retryAfter?: number } {
    21→  const now = Date.now()
    22→  const entry = rateLimitStore.get(ip)
    23→
    24→  if (!entry) {
    25→    return { allowed: true }
    26→  }
    27→
    28→  // Check if currently blocked
    29→  if (entry.blockedUntil && entry.blockedUntil > now) {
    30→    return {
    31→      allowed: false,
    32→      retryAfter: Math.ceil((entry.blockedUntil - now) / 1000)
    33→    }
    34→  }
    35→
    36→  // Check if window has expired - reset if so
    37→  if (now - entry.lastAttempt > RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS) {
    38→    rateLimitStore.delete(ip)
    39→    return { allowed: true }
    40→  }
    41→
    42→  return { allowed: true }
    43→}
    44→
    45→/**
    46→ * Record a failed authentication attempt for an IP
    47→ * Returns true if the IP is now blocked
    48→ */
    49→export function recordFailedAuth(ip: string): boolean {
    50→  const now = Date.now()
    51→  const entry = rateLimitStore.get(ip)
    52→
    53→  if (!entry) {
    54→    rateLimitStore.set(ip, {
    55→      attempts: 1,
    56→      lastAttempt: now,
    57→      blockedUntil: null
    58→    })
    59→    return false
    60→  }
    61→
    62→  // Reset if window has expired
    63→  if (now - entry.lastAttempt > RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS) {
    64→    rateLimitStore.set(ip, {
    65→      attempts: 1,
    66→      lastAttempt: now,
    67→      blockedUntil: null
    68→    })
    69→    return false
    70→  }
    71→
    72→  // Increment attempts
    73→  entry.attempts++
    74→  entry.lastAttempt = now
    75→
    76→  // Block if too many attempts
    77→  if (entry.attempts >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_ATTEMPTS) {
    78→    entry.blockedUntil = now + RATE_LIMIT_BLOCK_DURATION_MS
    79→    return true
    80→  }
    81→
    82→  return false
    83→}
    84→
    85→/**
    86→ * Clear rate limit for an IP (call on successful auth)
    87→ */
    88→export function clearRateLimit(ip: string): void {
    89→  rateLimitStore.delete(ip)
    90→}
    91→
    92→/**
    93→ * Get rate limit status for debugging
    94→ */
    95→export function getRateLimitStatus(ip: string): RateLimitEntry | null {
    96→  return rateLimitStore.get(ip) || null
    97→}
    98→
    99→export const authRateLimitConfig = {
   100→  RATE_LIMIT_MAX_ATTEMPTS,
   101→  RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS,
   102→  RATE_LIMIT_BLOCK_DURATION_MS
   103→} as const
   104→

1→/**
     2→ * Endpoint Rate Limiting Module
     3→ *
     4→ * Provides per-endpoint rate limiting for API requests.
     5→ */
     6→
     7→import type { EndpointRateLimitEntry } from './types.js'
     8→
     9→// Store: ip:endpoint -> { count, windowStart }
    10→const endpointRateLimitStore = new Map<string, EndpointRateLimitEntry>()
    11→
    12→// Default limits per endpoint category (requests per minute)
    13→const ENDPOINT_RATE_LIMITS: Record<string, number> = {
    14→  // Write operations are more restricted
    15→  'POST:/api/terminal/create': 10,
    16→  'POST:/api/pty/spawn': 10,
    17→  'POST:/api/pty/write': 120, // Higher limit for typing
    18→  'POST:/api/tts/speak': 30,
    19→  // Beads operations
    20→  'POST:/projects/beads': 30,
    21→  'PATCH:/projects/beads': 30,
    22→  'DELETE:/projects/beads': 20,
    23→  // Read operations have higher limits
    24→  'GET:/api': 60,
    25→  'GET:/projects': 60,
    26→  // Default fallback
    27→  default: 60
    28→}
    29→
    30→const ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS = 60 * 1000 // 1 minute window
    31→
    32→/**
    33→ * Get the rate limit for an endpoint
    34→ */
    35→function getEndpointRateLimit(method: string, path: string): number {
    36→  // Try exact match first
    37→  const exactKey = `${method}:${path}`
    38→  if (ENDPOINT_RATE_LIMITS[exactKey]) {
    39→    return ENDPOINT_RATE_LIMITS[exactKey]
    40→  }
    41→
    42→  // Try prefix matches
    43→  for (const [key, limit] of Object.entries(ENDPOINT_RATE_LIMITS)) {
    44→    if (exactKey.startsWith(key)) {
    45→      return limit
    46→    }
    47→  }
    48→
    49→  return ENDPOINT_RATE_LIMITS['default']
    50→}
    51→
    52→/**
    53→ * Check if a request is rate limited by endpoint
    54→ * Returns { allowed: boolean, remaining: number, resetIn: number }
    55→ */
    56→export function checkEndpointRateLimit(
    57→  ip: string,
    58→  method: string,
    59→  path: string
    60→): { allowed: boolean; remaining: number; resetIn: number } {
    61→  const now = Date.now()
    62→  const limit = getEndpointRateLimit(method, path)
    63→  const key = `${ip}:${method}:${path}`
    64→
    65→  const entry = endpointRateLimitStore.get(key)
    66→
    67→  if (!entry) {
    68→    // First request in this window
    69→    endpointRateLimitStore.set(key, { count: 1, windowStart: now })
    70→    return { allowed: true, remaining: limit - 1, resetIn: ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS }
    71→  }
    72→
    73→  // Check if window has expired
    74→  if (now - entry.windowStart >= ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS) {
    75→    // Start new window
    76→    endpointRateLimitStore.set(key, { count: 1, windowStart: now })
    77→    return { allowed: true, remaining: limit - 1, resetIn: ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS }
    78→  }
    79→
    80→  // Within window - check count
    81→  if (entry.count >= limit) {
    82→    const resetIn = ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS - (now - entry.windowStart)
    83→    return { allowed: false, remaining: 0, resetIn }
    84→  }
    85→
    86→  // Increment count
    87→  entry.count++
    88→  const remaining = limit - entry.count
    89→  const resetIn = ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS - (now - entry.windowStart)
    90→  return { allowed: true, remaining, resetIn }
    91→}
    92→
    93→/**
    94→ * Clean up expired endpoint rate limit entries
    95→ * Call periodically to prevent memory growth
    96→ */
    97→export function cleanupEndpointRateLimits(): void {
    98→  const now = Date.now()
    99→  for (const [key, entry] of endpointRateLimitStore.entries()) {
   100→    if (now - entry.windowStart >= ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS * 2) {
   101→      endpointRateLimitStore.delete(key)
   102→    }
   103→  }
   104→}
   105→
   106→export const endpointRateLimitConfig = {
   107→  ENDPOINT_RATE_LIMITS,
   108→  ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS
   109→} as const
   110→

1→/**
     2→ * WebSocket Manager - WebSocket handling
     3→ */
     4→
     5→import { WebSocket, WebSocketServer } from 'ws'
     6→import { Server } from 'http'
     7→import { log } from './utils'
     8→import { PendingFile } from './types'
     9→
    10→export interface WebSocketManagerDeps {
    11→  getToken: () => string
    12→  getPtyManager: () => any
    13→  getPort: () => number
    14→  getTerminalSubscriptions: () => Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
    15→  getPtyStreams: () => Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
    16→  getPtyDataBuffer: () => Map<string, string[]>
    17→  getConnectedClients: () => Set<WebSocket>
    18→  getPendingFiles: () => Map<string, PendingFile>
    19→}
    20→
    21→export function setupWebSocket(server: Server, deps: WebSocketManagerDeps): WebSocketServer {
    22→  const wss = new WebSocketServer({ noServer: true })
    23→
    24→  // Handle upgrade requests manually to support multiple paths
    25→  server.on('upgrade', (req, socket, head) => {
    26→    const url = new URL(req.url || '', `http://localhost:${deps.getPort()}`)
    27→    const pathname = url.pathname
    28→
    29→    // Get token from Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header (preferred) or query string (fallback)
    30→    const protocolHeader = req.headers['sec-websocket-protocol'] as string | undefined
    31→    const protocols = protocolHeader?.split(',').map(p => p.trim()) || []
    32→    const tokenFromProtocol = protocols.find(p => p.startsWith('token-'))?.slice(6)
    33→    const tokenFromQuery = url.searchParams.get('token')
    34→    const token = tokenFromProtocol || tokenFromQuery
    35→
    36→    log('WebSocket upgrade request', {
    37→      url: req.url,
    38→      pathname,
    39→      hasProtocolToken: !!tokenFromProtocol,
    40→      hasQueryToken: !!tokenFromQuery,
    41→      headers: {
    42→        host: req.headers.host,
    43→        origin: req.headers.origin,
    44→        upgrade: req.headers.upgrade
    45→      }
    46→    })
    47→
    48→    // Validate token for all WebSocket connections
    49→    if (token !== deps.getToken()) {
    50→      log('WebSocket auth failed', { providedToken: token?.slice(0, 8) })
    51→      socket.write('HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized\r\n\r\n')
    52→      socket.destroy()
    53→      return
    54→    }
    55→
    56→    // Handle PTY stream WebSocket: /api/pty/:id/stream
    57→    const ptyStreamMatch = pathname.match(/^\/api\/pty\/([^/]+)\/stream$/)
    58→    if (ptyStreamMatch) {
    59→      const ptyId = ptyStreamMatch[1]
    60→      handlePtyStreamUpgrade(req, socket, head, ptyId, deps)
    61→      return
    62→    }
    63→
    64→    // Handle main WebSocket path: /ws
    65→    if (pathname === '/ws') {
    66→      wss.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head, (ws) => {
    67→        wss.emit('connection', ws, req)
    68→      })
    69→      return
    70→    }
    71→
    72→    // Unknown WebSocket path
    73→    log('Unknown WebSocket path', { pathname })
    74→    socket.write('HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found\r\n\r\n')
    75→    socket.destroy()
    76→  })
    77→
    78→  wss.on('connection', (ws: WebSocket, _req) => {
    79→    log('WebSocket client connected (main)')
    80→
    81→    deps.getConnectedClients().add(ws)
    82→
    83→    ws.on('message', (message: Buffer) => {
    84→      try {
    85→        const msg = JSON.parse(message.toString())
    86→        handleWebSocketMessage(ws, msg, deps)
    87→      } catch (e) {
    88→        log('Invalid WebSocket message', { error: String(e) })
    89→      }
    90→    })
    91→
    92→    ws.on('close', () => {
    93→      deps.getConnectedClients().delete(ws)
    94→
    95→      deps.getTerminalSubscriptions().forEach((subscribers, ptyId) => {
    96→        subscribers.delete(ws)
    97→        if (subscribers.size === 0) {
    98→          deps.getTerminalSubscriptions().delete(ptyId)
    99→        }
   100→      })
   101→      log('WebSocket client disconnected (main)')
   102→    })
   103→
   104→    // Send welcome message
   105→    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'connected', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   106→
   107→    // Send any pending files to new client
   108→    sendPendingFilesToClient(ws, deps.getPendingFiles())
   109→  })
   110→
   111→  return wss
   112→}
   113→
   114→function handlePtyStreamUpgrade(
   115→  req: any,
   116→  socket: any,
   117→  head: any,
   118→  ptyId: string,
   119→  deps: WebSocketManagerDeps
   120→): void {
   121→  const ptyManager = deps.getPtyManager()
   122→  if (!ptyManager || !ptyManager.getProcess(ptyId)) {
   123→    log('PTY stream upgrade failed - PTY not found', { ptyId })
   124→    socket.write('HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found\r\n\r\n')
   125→    socket.destroy()
   126→    return
   127→  }
   128→
   129→  const streamWss = new WebSocketServer({ noServer: true })
   130→
   131→  streamWss.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head, (ws) => {
   132→    log('PTY stream connected', { ptyId })
   133→
   134→    const ptyStreams = deps.getPtyStreams()
   135→    if (!ptyStreams.has(ptyId)) {
   136→      ptyStreams.set(ptyId, new Set())
   137→    }
   138→    ptyStreams.get(ptyId)!.add(ws)
   139→
   140→    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'connected', ptyId }))
   141→
   142→    // Flush any buffered data
   143→    flushPtyBuffer(ptyId, ws, deps.getPtyDataBuffer())
   144→
   145→    ws.on('message', (message: Buffer) => {
   146→      try {
   147→        const msg = JSON.parse(message.toString())
   148→
   149→        switch (msg.type) {
   150→          case 'input':
   151→            if (msg.data && ptyManager) {
   152→              ptyManager.write(ptyId, msg.data)
   153→            }
   154→            break
   155→
   156→          case 'resize':
   157→            if (msg.cols && msg.rows && ptyManager) {
   158→              ptyManager.resize(ptyId, msg.cols, msg.rows)
   159→            }
   160→            break
   161→
   162→          case 'ping':
   163→            ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'pong', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   164→            break
   165→
   166→          default:
   167→            log('Unknown PTY stream message type', { type: msg.type, ptyId })
   168→        }
   169→      } catch (e) {
   170→        log('Invalid PTY stream message', { error: String(e), ptyId })
   171→      }
   172→    })
   173→
   174→    ws.on('close', () => {
   175→      log('PTY stream disconnected', { ptyId })
   176→      ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   177→      if (ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.size === 0) {
   178→        ptyStreams.delete(ptyId)
   179→      }
   180→    })
   181→
   182→    ws.on('error', (err) => {
   183→      log('PTY stream error', { error: String(err), ptyId })
   184→      ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   185→    })
   186→  })
   187→}
   188→
   189→function handleWebSocketMessage(ws: WebSocket, msg: any, deps: WebSocketManagerDeps): void {
   190→  const ptyManager = deps.getPtyManager()
   191→  const terminalSubscriptions = deps.getTerminalSubscriptions()
   192→
   193→  switch (msg.type) {
   194→    case 'subscribe':
   195→      if (msg.ptyId) {
   196→        if (!terminalSubscriptions.has(msg.ptyId)) {
   197→          terminalSubscriptions.set(msg.ptyId, new Set())
   198→        }
   199→        terminalSubscriptions.get(msg.ptyId)!.add(ws)
   200→        ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'subscribed', ptyId: msg.ptyId }))
   201→      }
   202→      break
   203→
   204→    case 'unsubscribe':
   205→      if (msg.ptyId) {
   206→        terminalSubscriptions.get(msg.ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   207→      }
   208→      break
   209→
   210→    case 'write':
   211→      if (msg.ptyId && msg.data && ptyManager) {

   213→      }
   214→      break

   216→    case 'resize':

   218→        ptyManager.resize(msg.ptyId, msg.cols, msg.rows)
   219→      }
   220→      break
   221→
   222→    case 'ping':
   223→      ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'pong', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   224→      break
   225→  }
   226→}

   228→function flushPtyBuffer(ptyId: string, ws: WebSocket, ptyDataBuffer: Map<string, string[]>): void {
   229→  const buffer = ptyDataBuffer.get(ptyId)

   234→      ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'data', data }))
   235→    }
   236→  })
   237→

   239→}

   241→function sendPendingFilesToClient(ws: WebSocket, pendingFiles: Map<string, PendingFile>): void {
   242→  const now = Date.now()
   243→  // Clean up expired files first
   244→  for (const [fileId, file] of pendingFiles) {
   245→    if (file.expiresAt < now) {
   246→      pendingFiles.delete(fileId)
   247→    }
   248→  }
   249→
   250→  const files = Array.from(pendingFiles.values()).map(f => ({
   251→    id: f.id,
   252→    name: f.name,
   253→    size: f.size,
   254→    mimeType: f.mimeType,
   255→    message: f.message
   256→  }))
   257→
   258→  if (files.length > 0) {
   259→    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'files:pending', files }))
   260→  }
   261→}
   262→
   263→export function broadcastTerminalData(
   264→  ptyId: string,
   265→  data: string,
   266→  terminalSubscriptions: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
   267→): void {
   268→  const subscribers = terminalSubscriptions.get(ptyId)
   269→  if (!subscribers) return

   274→    data
   275→  })

   277→  subscribers.forEach(ws => {
   278→    if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   279→      ws.send(message)
   280→    }
   281→  })
   282→}
   283→
   284→export function broadcastPtyData(
   285→  ptyId: string,

   288→  ptyDataBuffer: Map<string, string[]>
   289→): void {

   291→
   292→  // If no streams connected, buffer the data

   296→    }

     1→/**
     2→ * WebSocket Manager - WebSocket handling
     3→ */
     4→
     5→import { WebSocket, WebSocketServer } from 'ws'
     6→import { Server } from 'http'
     7→import { log } from './utils'
     8→import { PendingFile } from './types'
     9→
    10→export interface WebSocketManagerDeps {
    11→  getToken: () => string
    12→  getPtyManager: () => any
    13→  getPort: () => number
    14→  getTerminalSubscriptions: () => Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
    15→  getPtyStreams: () => Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
    16→  getPtyDataBuffer: () => Map<string, string[]>
    17→  getConnectedClients: () => Set<WebSocket>
    18→  getPendingFiles: () => Map<string, PendingFile>
    19→}
    20→
    21→export function setupWebSocket(server: Server, deps: WebSocketManagerDeps): WebSocketServer {
    22→  const wss = new WebSocketServer({ noServer: true })
    23→
    24→  // Handle upgrade requests manually to support multiple paths
    25→  server.on('upgrade', (req, socket, head) => {
    26→    const url = new URL(req.url || '', `http://localhost:${deps.getPort()}`)
    27→    const pathname = url.pathname
    28→
    29→    // Get token from Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header (preferred) or query string (fallback)
    30→    const protocolHeader = req.headers['sec-websocket-protocol'] as string | undefined
    31→    const protocols = protocolHeader?.split(',').map(p => p.trim()) || []
    32→    const tokenFromProtocol = protocols.find(p => p.startsWith('token-'))?.slice(6)
    33→    const tokenFromQuery = url.searchParams.get('token')
    34→    const token = tokenFromProtocol || tokenFromQuery
    35→
    36→    log('WebSocket upgrade request', {
    37→      url: req.url,
    38→      pathname,
    39→      hasProtocolToken: !!tokenFromProtocol,
    40→      hasQueryToken: !!tokenFromQuery,
    41→      headers: {
    42→        host: req.headers.host,
    43→        origin: req.headers.origin,
    44→        upgrade: req.headers.upgrade
    45→      }
    46→    })
    47→
    48→    // Validate token for all WebSocket connections
    49→    if (token !== deps.getToken()) {
    50→      log('WebSocket auth failed', { providedToken: token?.slice(0, 8) })
    51→      socket.write('HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized\r\n\r\n')
    52→      socket.destroy()
    53→      return
    54→    }
    55→
    56→    // Handle PTY stream WebSocket: /api/pty/:id/stream
    57→    const ptyStreamMatch = pathname.match(/^\/api\/pty\/([^/]+)\/stream$/)
    58→    if (ptyStreamMatch) {
    59→      const ptyId = ptyStreamMatch[1]
    60→      handlePtyStreamUpgrade(req, socket, head, ptyId, deps)
    61→      return
    62→    }
    63→
    64→    // Handle main WebSocket path: /ws
    65→    if (pathname === '/ws') {
    66→      wss.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head, (ws) => {
    67→        wss.emit('connection', ws, req)
    68→      })
    69→      return
    70→    }
    71→
    72→    // Unknown WebSocket path
    73→    log('Unknown WebSocket path', { pathname })
    74→    socket.write('HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found\r\n\r\n')
    75→    socket.destroy()
    76→  })
    77→
    78→  wss.on('connection', (ws: WebSocket, _req) => {
    79→    log('WebSocket client connected (main)')
    80→
    81→    deps.getConnectedClients().add(ws)
    82→
    83→    ws.on('message', (message: Buffer) => {
    84→      try {
    85→        const msg = JSON.parse(message.toString())
    86→        handleWebSocketMessage(ws, msg, deps)
    87→      } catch (e) {
    88→        log('Invalid WebSocket message', { error: String(e) })
    89→      }
    90→    })
    91→
    92→    ws.on('close', () => {
    93→      deps.getConnectedClients().delete(ws)
    94→
    95→      deps.getTerminalSubscriptions().forEach((subscribers, ptyId) => {
    96→        subscribers.delete(ws)
    97→        if (subscribers.size === 0) {
    98→          deps.getTerminalSubscriptions().delete(ptyId)
    99→        }
   100→      })
   101→      log('WebSocket client disconnected (main)')
   102→    })
   103→
   104→    // Send welcome message
   105→    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'connected', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   106→
   107→    // Send any pending files to new client
   108→    sendPendingFilesToClient(ws, deps.getPendingFiles())
   109→  })
   110→
   111→  return wss
   112→}
   113→
   114→function handlePtyStreamUpgrade(
   115→  req: any,
   116→  socket: any,
   117→  head: any,
   118→  ptyId: string,
   119→  deps: WebSocketManagerDeps
   120→): void {
   121→  const ptyManager = deps.getPtyManager()
   122→  if (!ptyManager || !ptyManager.getProcess(ptyId)) {
   123→    log('PTY stream upgrade failed - PTY not found', { ptyId })
   124→    socket.write('HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found\r\n\r\n')
   125→    socket.destroy()
   126→    return
   127→  }
   128→
   129→  const streamWss = new WebSocketServer({ noServer: true })
   130→
   131→  streamWss.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head, (ws) => {
   132→    log('PTY stream connected', { ptyId })
   133→
   134→    const ptyStreams = deps.getPtyStreams()
   135→    if (!ptyStreams.has(ptyId)) {
   136→      ptyStreams.set(ptyId, new Set())
   137→    }
   138→    ptyStreams.get(ptyId)!.add(ws)
   139→
   140→    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'connected', ptyId }))
   141→
   142→    // Flush any buffered data
   143→    flushPtyBuffer(ptyId, ws, deps.getPtyDataBuffer())
   144→
   145→    ws.on('message', (message: Buffer) => {
   146→      try {
   147→        const msg = JSON.parse(message.toString())
   148→
   149→        switch (msg.type) {
   150→          case 'input':
   151→            if (msg.data && ptyManager) {
   152→              ptyManager.write(ptyId, msg.data)
   153→            }
   154→            break
   155→
   156→          case 'resize':
   157→            if (msg.cols && msg.rows && ptyManager) {
   158→              ptyManager.resize(ptyId, msg.cols, msg.rows)
   159→            }
   160→            break
   161→
   162→          case 'ping':
   163→            ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'pong', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   164→            break
   165→
   166→          default:
   167→            log('Unknown PTY stream message type', { type: msg.type, ptyId })
   168→        }
   169→      } catch (e) {
   170→        log('Invalid PTY stream message', { error: String(e), ptyId })
   171→      }
   172→    })
   173→
   174→    ws.on('close', () => {
   175→      log('PTY stream disconnected', { ptyId })
   176→      ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   177→      if (ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.size === 0) {
   178→        ptyStreams.delete(ptyId)
   179→      }
   180→    })
   181→
   182→    ws.on('error', (err) => {
   183→      log('PTY stream error', { error: String(err), ptyId })
   184→      ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   185→    })
   186→  })
   187→}
   188→
   189→function handleWebSocketMessage(ws: WebSocket, msg: any, deps: WebSocketManagerDeps): void {
   190→  const ptyManager = deps.getPtyManager()
   191→  const terminalSubscriptions = deps.getTerminalSubscriptions()
   192→
   193→  switch (msg.type) {
   194→    case 'subscribe':

   198→        }

   201→      }
   202→      break

   205→      if (msg.ptyId) {

   209→
   210→    case 'write':
   211→      if (msg.ptyId && msg.data && ptyManager) {
   212→        ptyManager.write(msg.ptyId, msg.data)
   213→      }
   214→      break
   215→
   216→    case 'resize':
   217→      if (msg.ptyId && msg.cols && msg.rows && ptyManager) {
   218→        ptyManager.resize(msg.ptyId, msg.cols, msg.rows)
   219→      }
   220→      break
   221→
   222→    case 'ping':
   223→      ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'pong', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   224→      break
   225→  }
   226→}
   227→
   228→function flushPtyBuffer(ptyId: string, ws: WebSocket, ptyDataBuffer: Map<string, string[]>): void {
   229→  const buffer = ptyDataBuffer.get(ptyId)

    59→export class MobileServer {

   246→    certFingerprint: string
   247→    secure: boolean
   248→    nonce: string
   249→    nonceExpires: number
   250→    qrData: string
   251→  } {
   252→    const ips = getLocalIPs()
   253→    const primaryIp = ips[0] || 'localhost'
   254→    const fingerprint = getOrCreateFingerprint()
   255→    const { nonce, expiresAt } = createNonce()
   256→
   257→    const tailscaleHostname = getTailscaleHostname()
   258→    const allHosts = tailscaleHostname ? [...ips, tailscaleHostname] : ips
   259→
   260→    // QR version 3: adds TLS certificate pinning
   261→    // - certFingerprint: SHA256 of server's TLS certificate (for pinning)
   262→    // - secure: true means use HTTPS/WSS
   263→    const qrPayload = {

   265→      version: 3,

   271→      certFingerprint: this.certFingerprint, // TLS certificate fingerprint for pinning
   272→      secure: this.useTls,
   273→      nonce,
   274→      nonceExpires: expiresAt
   275→    }
   276→
   277→    const protocol = this.useTls ? 'https' : 'http'
   278→    return {
   279→      url: `claude-terminal://${primaryIp}:${this.port}?token=${this.token}`,
   280→      token: this.token,
   281→      port: this.port,

   285→      certFingerprint: this.certFingerprint,

   300→  }

   304→  }

   308→  }

   312→  }

   314→  async start(): Promise<void> {
   315→    try {
   316→      // Get TLS certificate (generates on first run)
   317→      if (this.useTls) {
   318→        log('Initializing TLS certificate...')
   319→        const tlsOptions = await getTlsOptions()
   320→        this.certFingerprint = await getCertificateFingerprint()
   321→        this.server = createHttpsServer(tlsOptions, this.app)
   322→        log(`TLS enabled, cert fingerprint: ${this.certFingerprint.slice(0, 16)}...`)
   323→      } else {
   324→        this.server = createHttpServer(this.app)
   325→        log('TLS disabled, using HTTP')
   326→      }
   327→
   328→      this.setupWebSocket()
   329→      startNonceCleanup()
   330→
   331→      this.rateLimitCleanupInterval = setInterval(() => {
   332→        cleanupEndpointRateLimits()
   333→      }, 2 * 60 * 1000)
   334→
   335→      return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
   336→        this.server!.listen(this.port, '0.0.0.0', () => {
   337→          const protocol = this.useTls ? 'HTTPS' : 'HTTP'
   338→          log(`Started ${protocol} server on port ${this.port}`)
   339→          log(`Token: ${this.token.slice(0, 8)}...`)
   340→          if (this.useTls) {
   341→            log(`Cert fingerprint: ${this.certFingerprint.slice(0, 32)}...`)
   342→          }
   343→          resolve()
   344→        })
   345→
   346→        this.server!.on('error', (err: NodeJS.ErrnoException) => {
   347→          if (err.code === 'EADDRINUSE') {
   348→            log(`Port ${this.port} in use, trying ${this.port + 1}`)
   349→            this.port++
   350→            this.server?.close()
   351→            this.start().then(resolve).catch(reject)
   352→          } else {
   353→            log('Server error', { error: String(err) })
   354→            reject(err)
   355→          }
   356→        })
   357→      })
   358→    } catch (error) {
   359→      log('Failed to start server', { error: String(error) })
   360→      throw error
   361→    }
   362→  }
   363→
   364→  stop(): void {
   365→    stopNonceCleanup()
   366→
   367→    if (this.rateLimitCleanupInterval) {
   368→      clearInterval(this.rateLimitCleanupInterval)
   369→      this.rateLimitCleanupInterval = null
   370→    }
   371→
   372→    if (this.wss) {
   373→      this.wss.close()
   374→      this.wss = null
   375→    }
   376→    if (this.server) {
   377→      this.server.close()
   378→      this.server = null
   379→    }
   380→    log('Stopped')
   381→  }
   382→
   383→  isRunning(): boolean {
   384→    return this.server !== null && this.server.listening
   385→  }
   386→}
   387→
   388→// Re-export types
   389→export * from './types'
   390→

   346→        this.server!.on('error', (err: NodeJS.ErrnoException) => {
   347→          if (err.code === 'EADDRINUSE') {
   348→            log(`Port ${this.port} in use, trying ${this.port + 1}`)
   349→            this.port++
   350→            this.server?.close()
   351→            this.start().then(resolve).catch(reject)
   352→          } else {
   353→            log('Server error', { error: String(err) })
   354→            reject(err)
   355→          }
   356→        })
   357→      })
   358→    } catch (error) {
   359→      log('Failed to start server', { error: String(error) })
   360→      throw error
   361→    }
   362→  }
   363→
   364→  stop(): void {
   365→    stopNonceCleanup()
   366→
   367→    if (this.rateLimitCleanupInterval) {
   368→      clearInterval(this.rateLimitCleanupInterval)
   369→      this.rateLimitCleanupInterval = null
   370→    }
   371→
   372→    if (this.wss) {
   373→      this.wss.close()
   374→      this.wss = null
   375→    }
   376→    if (this.server) {
   377→      this.server.close()
   378→      this.server = null
   379→    }
   380→    log('Stopped')
   381→  }
   382→
   383→  isRunning(): boolean {
   384→    return this.server !== null && this.server.listening
   385→  }
   386→}
   387→
   388→// Re-export types
   389→export * from './types'
   390→
```

     5→import { Express, Request, Response } from 'express'

     9→import { LocalPty } from '../types'
    10→
    11→export function setupPtyRoutes(
    12→  app: Express,
    13→  getPtyManager: () => any,
    14→  getLocalPtys: () => Map<string, LocalPty>,
    15→  getPtyStreams: () => Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>,
    16→  getPtyDataBuffer: () => Map<string, string[]>,
    17→  broadcastPtyData: (ptyId: string, data: string) => void,
    18→  broadcastPtyExit: (ptyId: string, code: number) => void
    19→): void {
    20→  // Spawn a new PTY
    21→  app.post('/api/pty/spawn', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
    22→    try {
    23→      const { projectPath, sessionId, model, backend } = req.body
    24→
    25→      if (!projectPath || typeof projectPath !== 'string') {
    26→        return res.status(400).json({ error: 'projectPath is required' })
    27→      }
    28→
    29→      const pathValidation = validateProjectPath(projectPath)
    30→      if (!pathValidation.valid) {
    31→        return res.status(400).json({ error: pathValidation.error })
    32→      }
    33→      const safeProjectPath = pathValidation.normalizedPath!
    34→
    35→      const ptyManager = getPtyManager()
    36→      if (!ptyManager) {
    37→        return res.status(500).json({ error: 'PTY manager not available' })
    38→      }
    39→
    40→      log('PTY spawn request', { projectPath: safeProjectPath, sessionId, model, backend })
    41→
    42→      const ptyId = ptyManager.spawn(
    43→        safeProjectPath,
    44→        sessionId,
    45→        undefined, // autoAcceptTools
    46→        undefined, // permissionMode
    47→        model,
    48→        backend
    49→      )
    50→
    51→      // Track this PTY for cleanup
    52→      const localPty: LocalPty = {
    53→        ptyId,
    54→        projectPath: safeProjectPath,
    55→        dataCallbacks: new Set(),
    56→        exitCallbacks: new Set()
    57→      }
    58→      getLocalPtys().set(ptyId, localPty)
    59→
    60→      // Set up data forwarding to WebSocket streams
    61→      ptyManager.onData(ptyId, (data: string) => {
    62→        broadcastPtyData(ptyId, data)
    63→      })
    64→
    65→      // Set up exit handler
    66→      ptyManager.onExit(ptyId, (code: number) => {
    67→        log('PTY exited', { ptyId, code })
    68→        broadcastPtyExit(ptyId, code)
    69→        getLocalPtys().delete(ptyId)
    70→        getPtyStreams().delete(ptyId)
    71→        getPtyDataBuffer().delete(ptyId)
    72→      })
    73→
    74→      log('PTY spawned', { ptyId, projectPath })
    75→      res.json({ ptyId })
    76→    } catch (error) {
    77→      log('PTY spawn error', { error: String(error) })
    78→      res.status(500).json({ error: String(error) })
    79→    }
    80→  })
    81→
    82→  // Write data to PTY
    83→  app.post('/api/pty/:id/write', (req: Request, res: Response) => {
    84→    try {
    85→      const { id } = req.params
    86→      const { data } = req.body
    87→
    88→      if (!data || typeof data !== 'string') {
    89→        return res.status(400).json({ error: 'data is required and must be a string' })
    90→      }
    91→
    92→      const ptyManager = getPtyManager()
    93→      if (!ptyManager) {
    94→        return res.status(500).json({ error: 'PTY manager not available' })
    95→      }
    96→
    97→      if (!ptyManager.getProcess(id)) {
    98→        return res.status(404).json({ error: 'PTY not found' })
    99→      }
   100→
   101→      ptyManager.write(id, data)
   102→      log('PTY write', { ptyId: id, dataLength: data.length })
   103→      res.json({ success: true })
   104→    } catch (error) {
   105→      log('PTY write error', { error: String(error) })
   106→      res.status(500).json({ error: String(error) })
   107→    }
   108→  })
   109→
   110→  // Resize PTY
   111→  app.post('/api/pty/:id/resize', (req: Request, res: Response) => {
   112→    try {
   113→      const { id } = req.params
   114→      const { cols, rows } = req.body
   115→
   116→      if (typeof cols !== 'number' || typeof rows !== 'number') {
   117→        return res.status(400).json({ error: 'cols and rows are required and must be numbers' })
   118→      }
   119→
   120→      if (cols < 1 || rows < 1 || cols > 500 || rows > 500) {
   121→        return res.status(400).json({ error: 'cols and rows must be between 1 and 500' })
   122→      }
   123→
   124→      const ptyManager = getPtyManager()
   125→      if (!ptyManager) {
   126→        return res.status(500).json({ error: 'PTY manager not available' })
   127→      }
   128→
   129→      if (!ptyManager.getProcess(id)) {
   130→        return res.status(404).json({ error: 'PTY not found' })
   131→      }
   132→
   133→      ptyManager.resize(id, cols, rows)
   134→      log('PTY resize', { ptyId: id, cols, rows })
   135→      res.json({ success: true })
   136→    } catch (error) {
   137→      log('PTY resize error', { error: String(error) })
   138→      res.status(500).json({ error: String(error) })
   139→    }
   140→  })
   141→
   142→  // Kill PTY
   143→  app.delete('/api/pty/:id', (req: Request, res: Response) => {
   144→    try {
   145→      const { id } = req.params
   146→
   147→      const ptyManager = getPtyManager()
   148→      if (!ptyManager) {
   149→        return res.status(500).json({ error: 'PTY manager not available' })
   150→      }
   151→
   152→      if (!ptyManager.getProcess(id)) {
   153→        return res.status(404).json({ error: 'PTY not found' })
   154→      }
   155→
   156→      ptyManager.kill(id)
   157→      getLocalPtys().delete(id)
   158→
   159→      // Close any WebSocket streams for this PTY
   160→      const streams = getPtyStreams().get(id)
   161→      if (streams) {
   162→        streams.forEach(ws => {
   163→          if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   164→            ws.close(1000, 'PTY killed')
   165→          }
   166→        })
   167→        getPtyStreams().delete(id)
   168→      }
   169→
   170→      log('PTY killed', { ptyId: id })
   171→      res.json({ success: true })
   172→    } catch (error) {
   173→      log('PTY kill error', { error: String(error) })
   174→      res.status(500).json({ error: String(error) })
   175→    }
   176→  })
   177→}

1→/**
     2→ * Mobile API Server - Express Application
     3→ *
     4→ * Main server setup that exposes desktop IPC functionality as HTTP/WebSocket endpoints.
     5→ * Enables mobile app communication with the Claude Terminal desktop application.
     6→ */
     7→
     8→import express, { Express, Request, Response, NextFunction } from 'express'
     9→import * as http from 'http'
    10→import cors from 'cors'
    11→import {
    12→  MobileApiServerConfig,
    13→  DEFAULT_SERVER_CONFIG,
    14→  ApiResponse
    15→} from './types'
    16→import {
    17→  authMiddleware,
    18→  generatePrimaryToken,
    19→  getPrimaryToken,
    20→  getConnectionInfo,
    21→  cleanupExpiredTokens
    22→} from './auth'
    23→import { createApiRouter } from './routes'
    24→import { WebSocketHandler } from './ws-handler'
    25→
    26→// =============================================================================
    27→// Server State
    28→// =============================================================================
    29→
    30→let serverInstance: http.Server | null = null
    31→let wsHandler: WebSocketHandler | null = null
    32→let cleanupInterval: NodeJS.Timeout | null = null
    33→
    34→// =============================================================================
    35→// Service Registrations (IPC Handler Adapters)
    36→// =============================================================================
    37→
    38→/**
    39→ * Service interfaces that bridge IPC handlers to HTTP endpoints
    40→ * These are set by the main process to provide access to existing functionality
    41→ */
    42→export interface ServerServices {
    43→  // Workspace/Project management
    44→  getWorkspace: () => Promise<any>
    45→  saveWorkspace: (workspace: any) => Promise<void>
    46→
    47→  // Settings
    48→  getSettings: () => Promise<any>
    49→  saveSettings: (settings: any) => Promise<void>
    50→
    51→  // Sessions
    52→  discoverSessions: (projectPath: string, backend?: 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider') => Promise<any[]>
    53→
    54→  // PTY management
    55→  spawnPty: (cwd: string, sessionId?: string, model?: string, backend?: 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider') => Promise<string>
    56→  writePty: (id: string, data: string) => void
    57→  resizePty: (id: string, cols: number, rows: number) => void
    58→  killPty: (id: string) => void
    59→  onPtyData: (id: string, callback: (data: string) => void) => () => void
    60→  onPtyExit: (id: string, callback: (code: number) => void) => () => void
    61→
    62→  // Beads task management
    63→  beadsCheck: (cwd: string) => Promise<any>
    64→  beadsInit: (cwd: string) => Promise<any>
    65→  beadsList: (cwd: string) => Promise<any>
    66→  beadsShow: (cwd: string, taskId: string) => Promise<any>
    67→  beadsCreate: (cwd: string, title: string, description?: string, priority?: number, type?: string, labels?: string) => Promise<any>
    68→  beadsComplete: (cwd: string, taskId: string) => Promise<any>
    69→  beadsDelete: (cwd: string, taskId: string) => Promise<any>
    70→  beadsStart: (cwd: string, taskId: string) => Promise<any>
    71→  beadsUpdate: (cwd: string, taskId: string, status?: string, title?: string, description?: string, priority?: number) => Promise<any>
    72→
    73→  // GSD progress
    74→  gsdProjectCheck: (cwd: string) => Promise<any>
    75→  gsdGetProgress: (cwd: string) => Promise<any>
    76→
    77→  // Voice (TTS)
    78→  voiceSpeak: (text: string) => Promise<any>
    79→  voiceStopSpeaking: () => Promise<any>
    80→  voiceGetSettings: () => Promise<any>
    81→
    82→  // CLI checks
    83→  claudeCheck: () => Promise<any>
    84→  geminiCheck: () => Promise<any>
    85→  codexCheck: () => Promise<any>
    86→  opencodeCheck: () => Promise<any>
    87→  aiderCheck: () => Promise<any>
    88→}
    89→
    90→let services: Partial<ServerServices> = {}
    91→
    92→/**
    93→ * Register services that provide IPC handler functionality
    94→ */
    95→export function registerServices(newServices: Partial<ServerServices>): void {
    96→  services = { ...services, ...newServices }
    97→}
    98→
    99→/**
   100→ * Get registered services
   101→ */
   102→export function getServices(): Partial<ServerServices> {
   103→  return services
   104→}
   105→
   106→// =============================================================================
   107→// Express App Factory
   108→// =============================================================================
   109→
   110→/**
   111→ * Create and configure the Express application
   112→ */
   113→export function createApp(config: MobileApiServerConfig = DEFAULT_SERVER_CONFIG): Express {
   114→  const app = express()
   115→
   116→  // ==========================================================================
   117→  // Middleware Stack
   118→  // ==========================================================================
   119→
   120→  // JSON body parsing with size limit
   121→  app.use(express.json({ limit: '100kb' }))
   122→
   123→  // CORS configuration
   124→  if (config.enableCors) {
   125→    app.use(cors({
   126→      origin: config.corsOrigins || '*',
   127→      methods: ['GET', 'POST', 'PUT', 'DELETE', 'PATCH', 'OPTIONS'],
   128→      allowedHeaders: ['Content-Type', 'Authorization'],
   129→      credentials: true
   130→    }))
   131→  }
   132→
   133→  // Request logging (development)
   134→  app.use((req: Request, _res: Response, next: NextFunction) => {
   135→    console.log(`[Mobile API] ${req.method} ${req.path}`)
   136→    next()
   137→  })
   138→
   139→  // ==========================================================================
   140→  // Health Check (unauthenticated)
   141→  // ==========================================================================
   142→
   143→  app.get('/health', (_req: Request, res: Response) => {
   144→    const response: ApiResponse<{ status: string; uptime: number }> = {
   145→      success: true,
   146→      data: {
   147→        status: 'healthy',
   148→        uptime: process.uptime()
   149→      },
   150→      timestamp: Date.now()
   151→    }
   152→    res.json(response)
   153→  })
   154→
   155→  // ==========================================================================
   156→  // Connection Info (unauthenticated - for initial pairing)
   157→  // ==========================================================================
   158→
   159→  app.get('/connect', (_req: Request, res: Response) => {
   160→    const token = getPrimaryToken()
   161→    if (!token) {
   162→      res.status(503).json({
   163→        success: false,
   164→        error: 'Server not fully initialized',
   165→        timestamp: Date.now()
   166→      })
   167→      return
   168→    }
   169→
   170→    // Return connection info for QR code
   171→    const info = getConnectionInfo(config.host, config.port)
   172→    res.json({
   173→      success: true,
   174→      data: {
   175→        version: 1,
   176→        wsEndpoint: `/ws`,
   177→        apiEndpoint: `/api`,
   178→        tokenHint: info.token.slice(0, 8) + '...' // Partial token for verification
   179→      },
   180→      timestamp: Date.now()
   181→    })
   182→  })
   183→
   184→  // ==========================================================================
   185→  // API Routes (authenticated)
   186→  // ==========================================================================
   187→
   188→  // Apply auth middleware to all /api routes
   189→  app.use('/api', authMiddleware as any)
   190→
   191→  // Mount the API router
   192→  app.use('/api', createApiRouter())
   193→
   194→  // ==========================================================================
   195→  // Error Handling
   196→  // ==========================================================================
   197→
   198→  // 404 handler
   199→  app.use((_req: Request, res: Response) => {
   200→    res.status(404).json({
   201→      success: false,
   202→      error: 'Endpoint not found',
   203→      timestamp: Date.now()
   204→    })
   205→  })
   206→
   207→  // Global error handler
   208→  app.use((err: Error, _req: Request, res: Response, _next: NextFunction) => {
   209→    console.error('[Mobile API] Error:', err)
   210→    res.status(500).json({
   211→      success: false,
   212→      error: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development' ? err.message : 'Internal server error',
   213→      timestamp: Date.now()
   214→    })
   215→  })
   216→
   217→  return app
   218→}
   219→
   220→// =============================================================================
   221→// Server Lifecycle
   222→// =============================================================================
   223→
   224→/**
   225→ * Start the mobile API server
   226→ */
   227→export function startServer(
   228→  config: Partial<MobileApiServerConfig> = {}
   229→): Promise<{ server: http.Server; wsHandler: WebSocketHandler; token: string }> {
   230→  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
   231→    const fullConfig: MobileApiServerConfig = { ...DEFAULT_SERVER_CONFIG, ...config }
   232→
   233→    // Stop existing server if running
   234→    if (serverInstance) {
   235→      stopServer()
   236→    }
   237→
   238→    // Generate primary auth token
   239→    const token = generatePrimaryToken()
   240→    console.log('[Mobile API] Generated auth token:', token.token.slice(0, 8) + '...')
   241→
   242→    // Create Express app
   243→    const app = createApp(fullConfig)
   244→
   245→    // Create HTTP server
   246→    serverInstance = http.createServer(app)
   247→
   248→    // Set up WebSocket handler if enabled
   249→    if (fullConfig.enableWebSocket) {
   250→      wsHandler = new WebSocketHandler(serverInstance, fullConfig.maxConnections)
   251→    }
   252→
   253→    // Start periodic cleanup of expired tokens
   254→    cleanupInterval = setInterval(() => {
   255→      const cleaned = cleanupExpiredTokens()
   256→      if (cleaned > 0) {
   257→        console.log(`[Mobile API] Cleaned up ${cleaned} expired tokens`)
   258→      }
   259→    }, 60000) // Every minute
   260→
   261→    // Start listening
   262→    serverInstance.listen(fullConfig.port, fullConfig.host, () => {
   263→      console.log(`[Mobile API] Server started on ${fullConfig.host}:${fullConfig.port}`)
   264→      console.log(`[Mobile API] WebSocket endpoint: ws://${fullConfig.host}:${fullConfig.port}/ws`)
   265→
   266→      resolve({
   267→        server: serverInstance!,
   268→        wsHandler: wsHandler!,
   269→        token: token.token
   270→      })
   271→    })
   272→
   273→    serverInstance.on('error', (error: NodeJS.ErrnoException) => {
   274→      console.error('[Mobile API] Server error:', error)
   275→      if (error.code === 'EADDRINUSE') {
   276→        reject(new Error(`Port ${fullConfig.port} is already in use`))
   277→      } else {
   278→        reject(error)
   279→      }
   280→    })
   281→  })
   282→}
   283→
   284→/**
   285→ * Stop the mobile API server
   286→ */
   287→export function stopServer(): Promise<void> {
   288→  return new Promise((resolve) => {
   289→    // Stop cleanup interval
   290→    if (cleanupInterval) {
   291→      clearInterval(cleanupInterval)
   292→      cleanupInterval = null
   293→    }
   294→
   295→    // Close WebSocket handler
   296→    if (wsHandler) {
   297→      wsHandler.closeAll()
   298→      wsHandler = null
   299→    }
   300→
   301→    // Close HTTP server
   302→    if (serverInstance) {
   303→      serverInstance.close(() => {
   304→        console.log('[Mobile API] Server stopped')
   305→        serverInstance = null
   306→        resolve()
   307→      })
   308→    } else {
   309→      resolve()
   310→    }
   311→  })
   312→}
   313→
   314→/**
   315→ * Check if server is running
   316→ */
   317→export function isServerRunning(): boolean {
   318→  return serverInstance !== null && serverInstance.listening
   319→}
   320→
   321→/**
   322→ * Get the WebSocket handler instance
   323→ */
   324→export function getWsHandler(): WebSocketHandler | null {
   325→  return wsHandler
   326→}
   327→
   328→/**
   329→ * Get server information
   330→ */
   331→export function getServerInfo(): {
   332→  running: boolean
   333→  port?: number
   334→  host?: string
   335→  token?: string
   336→  connections?: number
   337→} {
   338→  if (!serverInstance || !serverInstance.listening) {
   339→    return { running: false }
   340→  }
   341→
   342→  const address = serverInstance.address()
   343→  const token = getPrimaryToken()
   344→
   345→  return {
   346→    running: true,
   347→    port: typeof address === 'object' && address ? address.port : undefined,
   348→    host: typeof address === 'object' && address ? address.address : undefined,
   349→    token: token?.token,
   350→    connections: wsHandler?.getConnectionCount() ?? 0
   351→  }
   352→}

     1→/**
     2→ * Mobile API Server - Express Application
     3→ *
     4→ * Main server setup that exposes desktop IPC functionality as HTTP/WebSocket endpoints.
     5→ * Enables mobile app communication with the Claude Terminal desktop application.
     6→ */
     7→
     8→import express, { Express, Request, Response, NextFunction } from 'express'
     9→import * as http from 'http'
    10→import cors from 'cors'
    11→import {
    12→  MobileApiServerConfig,
    13→  DEFAULT_SERVER_CONFIG,
    14→  ApiResponse
    15→} from './types'
    16→import {
    17→  authMiddleware,
    18→  generatePrimaryToken,
    19→  getPrimaryToken,
    20→  getConnectionInfo,
    21→  cleanupExpiredTokens
    22→} from './auth'
    23→import { createApiRouter } from './routes'
    24→import { WebSocketHandler } from './ws-handler'
    25→
    26→// =============================================================================
    27→// Server State
    28→// =============================================================================
    29→
    30→let serverInstance: http.Server | null = null
    31→let wsHandler: WebSocketHandler | null = null
    32→let cleanupInterval: NodeJS.Timeout | null = null
    33→
    34→// =============================================================================
    35→// Service Registrations (IPC Handler Adapters)
    36→// =============================================================================
    37→
    38→/**
    39→ * Service interfaces that bridge IPC handlers to HTTP endpoints
    40→ * These are set by the main process to provide access to existing functionality
    41→ */
    42→export interface ServerServices {
    43→  // Workspace/Project management
    44→  getWorkspace: () => Promise<any>
    45→  saveWorkspace: (workspace: any) => Promise<void>
    46→
    47→  // Settings
    48→  getSettings: () => Promise<any>
    49→  saveSettings: (settings: any) => Promise<void>
    50→
    51→  // Sessions
    52→  discoverSessions: (projectPath: string, backend?: 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider') => Promise<any[]>
    53→
    54→  // PTY management
    55→  spawnPty: (cwd: string, sessionId?: string, model?: string, backend?: 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider') => Promise<string>
    56→  writePty: (id: string, data: string) => void
    57→  resizePty: (id: string, cols: number, rows: number) => void
    58→  killPty: (id: string) => void
    59→  onPtyData: (id: string, callback: (data: string) => void) => () => void
    60→  onPtyExit: (id: string, callback: (code: number) => void) => () => void
    61→
    62→  // Beads task management
    63→  beadsCheck: (cwd: string) => Promise<any>
    64→  beadsInit: (cwd: string) => Promise<any>
    65→  beadsList: (cwd: string) => Promise<any>
    66→  beadsShow: (cwd: string, taskId: string) => Promise<any>
    67→  beadsCreate: (cwd: string, title: string, description?: string, priority?: number, type?: string, labels?: string) => Promise<any>
    68→  beadsComplete: (cwd: string, taskId: string) => Promise<any>
    69→  beadsDelete: (cwd: string, taskId: string) => Promise<any>
    70→  beadsStart: (cwd: string, taskId: string) => Promise<any>
    71→  beadsUpdate: (cwd: string, taskId: string, status?: string, title?: string, description?: string, priority?: number) => Promise<any>
    72→
    73→  // GSD progress
    74→  gsdProjectCheck: (cwd: string) => Promise<any>
    75→  gsdGetProgress: (cwd: string) => Promise<any>
    76→
    77→  // Voice (TTS)
    78→  voiceSpeak: (text: string) => Promise<any>
    79→  voiceStopSpeaking: () => Promise<any>
    80→  voiceGetSettings: () => Promise<any>
    81→
    82→  // CLI checks
    83→  claudeCheck: () => Promise<any>
    84→  geminiCheck: () => Promise<any>
    85→  codexCheck: () => Promise<any>
    86→  opencodeCheck: () => Promise<any>
    87→  aiderCheck: () => Promise<any>
    88→}
    89→
    90→let services: Partial<ServerServices> = {}
    91→
    92→/**
    93→ * Register services that provide IPC handler functionality
    94→ */
    95→export function registerServices(newServices: Partial<ServerServices>): void {
    96→  services = { ...services, ...newServices }
    97→}
    98→
    99→/**
   100→ * Get registered services
   101→ */
   102→export function getServices(): Partial<ServerServices> {
   103→  return services
   104→}
   105→
   106→// =============================================================================
   107→// Express App Factory
   108→// =============================================================================
   109→
   110→/**
   111→ * Create and configure the Express application
   112→ */
   113→export function createApp(config: MobileApiServerConfig = DEFAULT_SERVER_CONFIG): Express {
   114→  const app = express()
   115→
   116→  // ==========================================================================
   117→  // Middleware Stack
   118→  // ==========================================================================
   119→
   120→  // JSON body parsing with size limit
   121→  app.use(express.json({ limit: '100kb' }))
   122→
   123→  // CORS configuration
   124→  if (config.enableCors) {
   125→    app.use(cors({
   126→      origin: config.corsOrigins || '*',
   127→      methods: ['GET', 'POST', 'PUT', 'DELETE', 'PATCH', 'OPTIONS'],
   128→      allowedHeaders: ['Content-Type', 'Authorization'],
   129→      credentials: true
   130→    }))
   131→  }
   132→
   133→  // Request logging (development)
   134→  app.use((req: Request, _res: Response, next: NextFunction) => {
   135→    console.log(`[Mobile API] ${req.method} ${req.path}`)
   136→    next()
   137→  })
   138→
   139→  // ==========================================================================
   140→  // Health Check (unauthenticated)
   141→  // ==========================================================================
   142→
   143→  app.get('/health', (_req: Request, res: Response) => {
   144→    const response: ApiResponse<{ status: string; uptime: number }> = {
   145→      success: true,
   146→      data: {
   147→        status: 'healthy',
   148→        uptime: process.uptime()
   149→      },
   150→      timestamp: Date.now()
   151→    }
   152→    res.json(response)
   153→  })
   154→
   155→  // ==========================================================================
   156→  // Connection Info (unauthenticated - for initial pairing)
   157→  // ==========================================================================
   158→
   159→  app.get('/connect', (_req: Request, res: Response) => {
   160→    const token = getPrimaryToken()
   161→    if (!token) {
   162→      res.status(503).json({
   163→        success: false,
   164→        error: 'Server not fully initialized',
   165→        timestamp: Date.now()
   166→      })
   167→      return
   168→    }
   169→
   170→    // Return connection info for QR code
   171→    const info = getConnectionInfo(config.host, config.port)
   172→    res.json({
   173→      success: true,
   174→      data: {
   175→        version: 1,
   176→        wsEndpoint: `/ws`,
   177→        apiEndpoint: `/api`,
   178→        tokenHint: info.token.slice(0, 8) + '...' // Partial token for verification
   179→      },
   180→      timestamp: Date.now()
   181→    })
   182→  })
   183→
   184→  // ==========================================================================
   185→  // API Routes (authenticated)
   186→  // ==========================================================================
   187→
   188→  // Apply auth middleware to all /api routes
   189→  app.use('/api', authMiddleware as any)
   190→
   191→  // Mount the API router
   192→  app.use('/api', createApiRouter())
   193→
   194→  // ==========================================================================
   195→  // Error Handling
   196→  // ==========================================================================
   197→
   198→  // 404 handler
   199→  app.use((_req: Request, res: Response) => {
   200→    res.status(404).json({
   201→      success: false,
   202→      error: 'Endpoint not found',
   203→      timestamp: Date.now()
   204→    })
   205→  })
   206→
   207→  // Global error handler
   208→  app.use((err: Error, _req: Request, res: Response, _next: NextFunction) => {
   209→    console.error('[Mobile API] Error:', err)
   210→    res.status(500).json({
   211→      success: false,
   212→      error: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development' ? err.message : 'Internal server error',
   213→      timestamp: Date.now()
   214→    })
   215→  })
   216→
   217→  return app
   218→}
   219→
   220→// =============================================================================
   221→// Server Lifecycle
   222→// =============================================================================
   223→
   224→/**
   225→ * Start the mobile API server
   226→ */
   227→export function startServer(
   228→  config: Partial<MobileApiServerConfig> = {}
   229→): Promise<{ server: http.Server; wsHandler: WebSocketHandler; token: string }> {
   230→  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
   231→    const fullConfig: MobileApiServerConfig = { ...DEFAULT_SERVER_CONFIG, ...config }
   232→
   233→    // Stop existing server if running
   234→    if (serverInstance) {
   235→      stopServer()
   236→    }
   237→
   238→    // Generate primary auth token
   239→    const token = generatePrimaryToken()
   240→    console.log('[Mobile API] Generated auth token:', token.token.slice(0, 8) + '...')
   241→
   242→    // Create Express app
   243→    const app = createApp(fullConfig)
   244→
   245→    // Create HTTP server
   246→    serverInstance = http.createServer(app)
   247→
   248→    // Set up WebSocket handler if enabled
   249→    if (fullConfig.enableWebSocket) {
   250→      wsHandler = new WebSocketHandler(serverInstance, fullConfig.maxConnections)
   251→    }
   252→
   253→    // Start periodic cleanup of expired tokens
   254→    cleanupInterval = setInterval(() => {
   255→      const cleaned = cleanupExpiredTokens()
   256→      if (cleaned > 0) {
   257→        console.log(`[Mobile API] Cleaned up ${cleaned} expired tokens`)
   258→      }
   259→    }, 60000) // Every minute
   260→
   261→    // Start listening
   262→    serverInstance.listen(fullConfig.port, fullConfig.host, () => {
   263→      console.log(`[Mobile API] Server started on ${fullConfig.host}:${fullConfig.port}`)
   264→      console.log(`[Mobile API] WebSocket endpoint: ws://${fullConfig.host}:${fullConfig.port}/ws`)
   265→
   266→      resolve({
   267→        server: serverInstance!,
   268→        wsHandler: wsHandler!,
   269→        token: token.token
   270→      })
   271→    })
   272→
   273→    serverInstance.on('error', (error: NodeJS.ErrnoException) => {
   274→      console.error('[Mobile API] Server error:', error)
   275→      if (error.code === 'EADDRINUSE') {
   276→        reject(new Error(`Port ${fullConfig.port} is already in use`))
   277→      } else {
   278→        reject(error)
   279→      }
   280→    })
   281→  })
   282→}
   283→
   284→/**
   285→ * Stop the mobile API server
   286→ */
   287→export function stopServer(): Promise<void> {
   288→  return new Promise((resolve) => {
   289→    // Stop cleanup interval
   290→    if (cleanupInterval) {
   291→      clearInterval(cleanupInterval)
   292→      cleanupInterval = null
   293→    }
   294→
   295→    // Close WebSocket handler
   296→    if (wsHandler) {
   297→      wsHandler.closeAll()
   298→      wsHandler = null
   299→    }
   300→
   301→    // Close HTTP server
   302→    if (serverInstance) {
   303→      serverInstance.close(() => {
   304→        console.log('[Mobile API] Server stopped')
   305→        serverInstance = null
   306→        resolve()
   307→      })
   308→    } else {
   309→      resolve()
   310→    }
   311→  })
   312→}
   313→
   314→/**
   315→ * Check if server is running
   316→ */
   317→export function isServerRunning(): boolean {
   318→  return serverInstance !== null && serverInstance.listening
   319→}
   320→
   321→/**
   322→ * Get the WebSocket handler instance
   323→ */
   324→export function getWsHandler(): WebSocketHandler | null {
   325→  return wsHandler
   326→}
   327→
   328→/**
   329→ * Get server information
   330→ */
   331→export function getServerInfo(): {
   332→  running: boolean
   333→  port?: number
   334→  host?: string
   335→  token?: string
   336→  connections?: number
   337→} {
   338→  if (!serverInstance || !serverInstance.listening) {
   339→    return { running: false }
   340→  }
   341→
   342→  const address = serverInstance.address()
   343→  const token = getPrimaryToken()
   344→
   345→  return {
   346→    running: true,
   347→    port: typeof address === 'object' && address ? address.port : undefined,
   348→    host: typeof address === 'object' && address ? address.address : undefined,
   349→    token: token?.token,
   350→    connections: wsHandler?.getConnectionCount() ?? 0
   351→  }
   352→}
   353→

   120→    let newId: string
   121→    try {
   122→      newId = ptyManager.spawn(cwd, effectiveSessionId, autoAcceptTools, permissionMode, undefined, newBackend)
   123→    } catch (error: any) {

   125→      // Clean up stale map entries from the killed PTY
   126→      ptyToProject.delete(oldId)
   127→      ptyToBackend.delete(oldId)

```
     1→import { BrowserWindow, ipcMain } from 'electron'
     2→import { PtyManager } from '../../pty-manager.js'
     3→import { SessionStore } from '../../session-store.js'
     4→import { ApiServerManager } from '../../api-server.js'
     5→import { pendingApiPrompts, autoCloseSessions } from '../api-prompt-handler.js'
     6→
     7→function maybeRespondToCursorPositionRequest(
     8→  ptyManager: PtyManager,
     9→  ptyToBackend: Map<string, string>,
    10→  ptyId: string,
    11→  data: string
    12→): void {
    13→  const backend = ptyToBackend.get(ptyId)
    14→  if (!backend || backend === 'claude') return
    15→  if (data.includes('\x1b[6n') || data.includes('\x1b[?6n')) {
    16→    ptyManager.write(ptyId, '\x1b[1;1R')
    17→  }
    18→}
    19→
    20→export function registerPtyHandlers(
    21→  ptyManager: PtyManager,
    22→  sessionStore: SessionStore,
    23→  apiServerManager: ApiServerManager,
    24→  ptyToProject: Map<string, string>,
    25→  ptyToBackend: Map<string, string>,
    26→  getMainWindow: () => BrowserWindow | null
    27→): void {
    28→  ipcMain.handle('pty:spawn', (_, { cwd, sessionId, model, backend }: { cwd: string; sessionId?: string; model?: string; backend?: 'default' | 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider' }) => {
    29→    try {
    30→      const workspace = sessionStore.getWorkspace()
    31→      const project = workspace.projects.find(p => p.path === cwd)
    32→      const globalSettings = sessionStore.getSettings()
    33→
    34→      const normalizedGlobalBackend = globalSettings.backend === 'default'
    35→        ? undefined
    36→        : globalSettings.backend
    37→
    38→      const normalizedBackend = backend === 'default' ? undefined : backend
    39→      const effectiveBackend = normalizedBackend
    40→        || (project?.backend && project.backend !== 'default'
    41→          ? project.backend
    42→          : normalizedGlobalBackend || 'claude')
    43→
    44→      const pending = pendingApiPrompts.get(cwd)
    45→      const effectiveModel = model || pending?.model
    46→      const autoAcceptTools = project?.autoAcceptTools ?? globalSettings.autoAcceptTools
    47→      const permissionMode = project?.permissionMode ?? globalSettings.permissionMode
    48→
    49→      const id = ptyManager.spawn(cwd, sessionId, autoAcceptTools, permissionMode, effectiveModel, effectiveBackend)
    50→      ptyToProject.set(id, cwd)
    51→      ptyToBackend.set(id, effectiveBackend)
    52→
    53→      if (project?.apiPort && project.apiAutoStart && !apiServerManager.isRunning(cwd)) {
    54→        apiServerManager.start(cwd, project.apiPort)
    55→      }
    56→
    57→      const mainWindow = getMainWindow()
    58→
    59→      ptyManager.onData(id, (data) => {
    60→        maybeRespondToCursorPositionRequest(ptyManager, ptyToBackend, id, data)
    61→        try {
    62→          mainWindow?.webContents.send(`pty:data:${id}`, data)
    63→        } catch (e) {
    64→          console.error('IPC send failed', e)
    65→        }
    66→      })
    67→
    68→      ptyManager.onExit(id, (code) => {
    69→        try {
    70→          mainWindow?.webContents.send(`pty:exit:${id}`, code)
    71→        } catch (e) {
    72→          console.error('IPC send failed', e)
    73→        }
    74→        ptyToProject.delete(id)
    75→        ptyToBackend.delete(id)
    76→        autoCloseSessions.delete(id)
    77→      })
    78→
    79→      if (pending) {
    80→        pendingApiPrompts.delete(cwd)
    81→        if (pending.autoClose) autoCloseSessions.add(id)
    82→        setTimeout(() => {
    83→          ptyManager.write(id, pending.prompt + '\n')
    84→          pending.resolve({ success: true, message: 'Prompt sent to new terminal', sessionCreated: true })
    85→        }, 4000)
    86→      }
    87→
    88→      return id
    89→    } catch (error: any) {
    90→      console.error('Failed to spawn PTY:', error)
    91→      throw new Error(`Failed to start Claude: ${error.message}`)
    92→    }
    93→  })
    94→
    95→  ipcMain.on('pty:write', (_, { id, data }: { id: string; data: string }) => ptyManager.write(id, data))
    96→  ipcMain.on('pty:resize', (_, { id, cols, rows }: { id: string; cols: number; rows: number }) => ptyManager.resize(id, cols, rows))
    97→  ipcMain.on('pty:kill', (_, id: string) => {
    98→    ptyToProject.delete(id)
    99→    ptyToBackend.delete(id)
   100→    ptyManager.kill(id)
   101→  })
   102→
   103→  ipcMain.handle('pty:set-backend', async (_, { id: oldId, backend: newBackend }: { id: string; backend: 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider' }) => {
   104→    const proc = ptyManager.getProcess(oldId)
   105→    if (!proc) return
   106→
   107→    const { cwd, sessionId, backend: oldBackend } = proc
   108→    const effectiveSessionId = oldBackend !== newBackend ? undefined : sessionId
   109→
   110→    // Look up project settings so the new PTY inherits permissions and model
   111→    const workspace = sessionStore.getWorkspace()
   112→    const project = workspace.projects.find(p => p.path === cwd)
   113→    const globalSettings = sessionStore.getSettings()
   114→    const autoAcceptTools = project?.autoAcceptTools ?? globalSettings.autoAcceptTools
   115→    const permissionMode = project?.permissionMode ?? globalSettings.permissionMode
   116→
   117→    // Kill old PTY first, then spawn new one with error recovery
   118→    ptyManager.kill(oldId)
   119→
   120→    let newId: string
   121→    try {
   122→      newId = ptyManager.spawn(cwd, effectiveSessionId, autoAcceptTools, permissionMode, undefined, newBackend)
   123→    } catch (error: any) {
   124→      console.error('Failed to spawn new PTY during backend switch:', error)
   125→      // Clean up stale map entries from the killed PTY
   126→      ptyToProject.delete(oldId)
   127→      ptyToBackend.delete(oldId)
   128→      autoCloseSessions.delete(oldId)
   129→      throw new Error(`Failed to switch backend to ${newBackend}: ${error.message}`)
   130→    }
   131→
   132→    const projectPath = ptyToProject.get(oldId)
   133→    if (projectPath) {
   134→      ptyToProject.set(newId, projectPath)
   135→      ptyToProject.delete(oldId)
   136→    }
   137→    ptyToBackend.set(newId, newBackend)
   138→    ptyToBackend.delete(oldId)
   139→
   140→    // Transfer autoClose flag if the old session had it
   141→    if (autoCloseSessions.delete(oldId)) {
   142→      autoCloseSessions.add(newId)
   143→    }
   144→
   145→    const mainWindow = getMainWindow()
   146→
   147→    ptyManager.onData(newId, (data) => {
   148→      maybeRespondToCursorPositionRequest(ptyManager, ptyToBackend, newId, data)
   149→      try {
   150→        mainWindow?.webContents.send(`pty:data:${newId}`, data)
   151→      } catch (e) {
   152→        console.error('IPC send failed', e)
   153→      }
   154→    })
   155→
   156→    ptyManager.onExit(newId, (code) => {
   157→      try {
   158→        mainWindow?.webContents.send(`pty:exit:${newId}`, code)
   159→      } catch (e) {
   160→        console.error('IPC send failed', e)
   161→      }
   162→      ptyToProject.delete(newId)
   163→      ptyToBackend.delete(newId)
   164→      autoCloseSessions.delete(newId)
   165→    })
   166→
   167→    mainWindow?.webContents.send('pty:recreated', { oldId, newId, backend: newBackend })
   168→  })
   169→}
   170→
