1→/**
     2→ * PTY WebSocket Management
     3→ *
     4→ * Manages WebSocket connections for PTY data streaming.
     5→ */
     6→
     7→import { MAX_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS, RECONNECT_DELAYS } from './constants'
     8→import { PtyWebSocketState } from './types'
     9→
    10→export class PtyWebSocketManager {
    11→  private ptyWebsockets: Map<string, PtyWebSocketState> = new Map()
    12→  private wsBaseUrl: string
    13→  private token: string
    14→
    15→  constructor(wsBaseUrl: string, token: string) {
    16→    this.wsBaseUrl = wsBaseUrl
    17→    this.token = token
    18→  }
    19→
    20→  getPtyWebsockets(): Map<string, PtyWebSocketState> {
    21→    return this.ptyWebsockets
    22→  }
    23→
    24→  /**
    25→   * Connect WebSocket for PTY data streaming
    26→   */
    27→  connectPtyStream(ptyId: string): void {
    28→    // Don't reconnect if already connected or connecting
    29→    const existing = this.ptyWebsockets.get(ptyId)
    30→    if (
    31→      existing?.ws &&
    32→      (existing.ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN || existing.ws.readyState === WebSocket.CONNECTING)
    33→    ) {
    34→      return
    35→    }
    36→
    37→    const url = `${this.wsBaseUrl}/api/pty/${ptyId}/stream?token=${encodeURIComponent(this.token)}`
    38→    console.log('[HttpBackend] Connecting PTY stream:', ptyId)
    39→
    40→    const ws = new WebSocket(url)
    41→
    42→    // Reuse existing state if available (preserves callbacks), otherwise create new
    43→    const state: PtyWebSocketState = existing || {
    44→      ws,
    45→      dataCallbacks: new Set(),
    46→      exitCallbacks: new Set(),
    47→      reconnectAttempts: 0,
    48→      reconnectTimer: null,
    49→      dataBuffer: []
    50→    }
    51→    // Update the WebSocket reference
    52→    state.ws = ws
    53→    state.reconnectAttempts = existing?.reconnectAttempts || 0
    54→
    55→    ws.onopen = () => {
    56→      console.log('[HttpBackend] PTY stream connected:', ptyId)
    57→      state.reconnectAttempts = 0
    58→    }
    59→
    60→    ws.onmessage = (event) => {
    61→      try {
    62→        const msg = JSON.parse(event.data)
    63→
    64→        switch (msg.type) {
    65→          case 'data':
    66→            // Buffer data if no callbacks registered yet
    67→            if (state.dataCallbacks.size === 0) {
    68→              state.dataBuffer.push(msg.data)
    69→              // Limit buffer size to prevent memory issues
    70→              if (state.dataBuffer.length > 1000) {
    71→                state.dataBuffer.shift()
    72→              }
    73→            } else {
    74→              // Forward data to all callbacks
    75→              state.dataCallbacks.forEach((cb) => cb(msg.data))
    76→            }
    77→            break
    78→
    79→          case 'exit':
    80→            // Forward exit to all callbacks
    81→            state.exitCallbacks.forEach((cb) => cb(msg.code))
    82→            // Clean up after exit
    83→            this.ptyWebsockets.delete(ptyId)
    84→            break
    85→
    86→          case 'connected':
    87→            console.log(
    88→              '[HttpBackend] PTY stream confirmed:',
    89→              ptyId,
    90→              'callbacks:',
    91→              state.dataCallbacks.size,
    92→              'buffered:',
    93→              state.dataBuffer.length
    94→            )
    95→            break
    96→
    97→          case 'pong':
    98→            // Keep-alive response, nothing to do
    99→            break
   100→
   101→          default:
   102→            console.log('[HttpBackend] Unknown PTY message type:', msg.type)
   103→        }
   104→      } catch (e) {
   105→        console.error('[HttpBackend] Failed to parse PTY message:', e)
   106→      }
   107→    }
   108→
   109→    ws.onerror = (event) => {
   110→      console.error('[HttpBackend] PTY stream error:', ptyId, event)
   111→    }
   112→
   113→    ws.onclose = (event) => {
   114→      console.log('[HttpBackend] PTY stream closed:', ptyId, event.code, event.reason)
   115→
   116→      // Attempt reconnection if not a clean close and we still want this PTY
   117→      if (!event.wasClean && state.reconnectAttempts < MAX_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS) {
   118→        const delay =
   119→          RECONNECT_DELAYS[Math.min(state.reconnectAttempts, RECONNECT_DELAYS.length - 1)]
   120→        console.log(`[HttpBackend] Reconnecting PTY stream in ${delay}ms...`)
   121→
   122→        state.reconnectTimer = setTimeout(() => {
   123→          state.reconnectAttempts++
   124→          this.connectPtyStream(ptyId)
   125→        }, delay)
   126→      } else if (event.code !== 1000) {
   127→        // Exit callback for abnormal closure
   128→        state.exitCallbacks.forEach((cb) => cb(-1))
   129→        this.ptyWebsockets.delete(ptyId)
   130→      }
   131→    }
   132→
   133→    this.ptyWebsockets.set(ptyId, state)
   134→  }
   135→
   136→  /**
   137→   * Disconnect PTY WebSocket stream
   138→   */
   139→  disconnectPtyStream(ptyId: string): void {
   140→    const state = this.ptyWebsockets.get(ptyId)
   141→    if (!state) return
   142→
   143→    // Clear reconnect timer
   144→    if (state.reconnectTimer) {
   145→      clearTimeout(state.reconnectTimer)
   146→    }
   147→
   148→    // Close WebSocket
   149→    if (
   150→      state.ws &&
   151→      (state.ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN || state.ws.readyState === WebSocket.CONNECTING)
   152→    ) {
   153→      state.ws.close(1000, 'Client requested close')
   154→    }
   155→
   156→    this.ptyWebsockets.delete(ptyId)
   157→  }
   158→
   159→  /**
   160→   * Disconnect all PTY WebSocket connections
   161→   */
   162→  disconnectAll(): void {
   163→    this.ptyWebsockets.forEach((state, ptyId) => {
   164→      if (state.reconnectTimer) {
   165→        clearTimeout(state.reconnectTimer)
   166→      }
   167→      if (
   168→        state.ws &&
   169→        (state.ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN || state.ws.readyState === WebSocket.CONNECTING)
   170→      ) {
   171→        state.ws.close(1000, 'Client disconnecting')
   172→      }
   173→    })
   174→    this.ptyWebsockets.clear()
   175→  }
   176→
   177→  /**
   178→   * Send data to a PTY via WebSocket
   179→   */
   180→  sendToPty(ptyId: string, message: object): boolean {
   181→    const state = this.ptyWebsockets.get(ptyId)
   182→    if (state && state.ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   183→      state.ws.send(JSON.stringify(message))
   184→      return true
   185→    }
   186→    return false
   187→  }
   188→}
   189→

```
   180→  sendToPty(ptyId: string, message: object): boolean {

   182→    if (state && state.ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   183→      state.ws.send(JSON.stringify(message))
   184→      return true
   185→    }
   186→    return false
   187→  }
   188→}
   189→
```

1→/**
     2→ * HTTP/WebSocket Backend Implementation
     3→ *
     4→ * This module implements the Api interface using HTTP requests and WebSocket
     5→ * connections to communicate with the mobile server running on a desktop host.
     6→ */
     7→
     8→import {
     9→  Api,
    10→  ConnectionState,
    11→  Settings,
    12→  Workspace,
    13→  Session,
    14→  PtyDataCallback,
    15→  PtyExitCallback,
    16→  PtyRecreatedCallback,
    17→  ApiOpenSessionCallback,
    18→  Unsubscribe,
    19→  BackendId
    20→} from '../types'
    21→import { HttpBackendConfig } from './types'
    22→import { DEFAULT_PORT } from './constants'
    23→import { isLocalNetwork } from './helpers'
    24→import { ConnectionManager } from './connection'
    25→import { PtyWebSocketManager } from './pty-websocket'
    26→import { PtyApi } from './pty-api'
    27→import { WorkspaceApi } from './workspace-api'
    28→
    29→export class HttpBackend implements Api {
    30→  voiceGetInstalled?: () => Promise<
    31→    Array<{
    32→      key: string
    33→      displayName: string
    34→      source: 'builtin' | 'downloaded' | 'custom'
    35→      quality?: string
    36→      language?: string
    37→    }>
    38→  >
    39→  xttsGetVoices?: () => Promise<
    40→    Array<{ id: string; name: string; language: string; createdAt: number }>
    41→  >
    42→  voiceGetSettings?: () => Promise<{ ttsVoice?: string; ttsEngine?: string }>
    43→  apiStart?: (
    44→    projectPath: string,
    45→    port: number
    46→  ) => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
    47→  apiStop?: (projectPath: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean }>
    48→
    49→  private host: string
    50→  private port: number
    51→  private connection: ConnectionManager
    52→  private wsManager: PtyWebSocketManager
    53→  private ptyApi: PtyApi
    54→  private workspaceApi: WorkspaceApi
    55→
    56→  constructor(config: HttpBackendConfig) {
    57→    // Validate port to prevent requests to invalid addresses like localhost:1
    58→    if (
    59→      !config.port ||
    60→      config.port < 1 ||
    61→      config.port > 65535 ||
    62→      !Number.isInteger(config.port)
    63→    ) {
    64→      console.error('[HttpBackend] Invalid port:', config.port, '- using default', DEFAULT_PORT)
    65→      config.port = DEFAULT_PORT
    66→    }
    67→    if (config.port < 1024) {
    68→      console.warn(
    69→        '[HttpBackend] Port',
    70→        config.port,
    71→        'is a privileged port (< 1024), this may fail'
    72→      )
    73→    }
    74→
    75→    this.host = config.host
    76→    this.port = config.port
    77→
    78→    // Determine protocol based on whether host is local
    79→    const httpProtocol = isLocalNetwork(config.host) ? 'http' : 'https'
    80→    const wsProtocol = isLocalNetwork(config.host) ? 'ws' : 'wss'
    81→
    82→    const baseUrl = `${httpProtocol}://${config.host}:${config.port}`
    83→    const wsBaseUrl = `${wsProtocol}://${config.host}:${config.port}`
    84→
    85→    console.log('[HttpBackend] Initialized with baseUrl:', baseUrl)
    86→
    87→    // Initialize managers
    88→    this.connection = new ConnectionManager(baseUrl, config.token)
    89→    this.wsManager = new PtyWebSocketManager(wsBaseUrl, config.token)
    90→    this.ptyApi = new PtyApi(this.connection, this.wsManager)
    91→    this.workspaceApi = new WorkspaceApi(this.connection)
    92→  }
    93→
    94→  // Connection State Management
    95→
    96→  getConnectionState(): ConnectionState {
    97→    return this.connection.getConnectionState()
    98→  }
    99→
   100→  getConnectionError(): string | null {
   101→    return this.connection.getConnectionError()
   102→  }
   103→
   104→  onConnectionStateChange(callback: (state: ConnectionState) => void): Unsubscribe {
   105→    return this.connection.onConnectionStateChange(callback)
   106→  }
   107→
   108→  // PTY Management
   109→
   110→  spawnPty(cwd: string, sessionId?: string, model?: string, backend?: BackendId): Promise<string> {
   111→    return this.ptyApi.spawnPty(cwd, sessionId, model, backend)
   112→  }
   113→
   114→  killPty(id: string): void {
   115→    this.ptyApi.killPty(id)
   116→  }
   117→
   118→  writePty(id: string, data: string): void {
   119→    this.ptyApi.writePty(id, data)
   120→  }
   121→
   122→  resizePty(id: string, cols: number, rows: number): void {
   123→    this.ptyApi.resizePty(id, cols, rows)
   124→  }
   125→
   126→  onPtyData(id: string, callback: PtyDataCallback): Unsubscribe {
   127→    return this.ptyApi.onPtyData(id, callback)
   128→  }
   129→
   130→  onPtyExit(id: string, callback: PtyExitCallback): Unsubscribe {
   131→    return this.ptyApi.onPtyExit(id, callback)
   132→  }
   133→
   134→  onPtyRecreated(callback: PtyRecreatedCallback): Unsubscribe {
   135→    return this.ptyApi.onPtyRecreated(callback)
   136→  }
   137→
   138→  // Session Management
   139→
   140→  discoverSessions(projectPath: string, backend?: BackendId): Promise<Session[]> {
   141→    return this.workspaceApi.discoverSessions(projectPath, backend)
   142→  }
   143→
   144→  // Workspace Management
   145→
   146→  getWorkspace(): Promise<Workspace> {
   147→    return this.workspaceApi.getWorkspace()
   148→  }
   149→
   150→  saveWorkspace(workspace: Workspace): Promise<void> {
   151→    return this.workspaceApi.saveWorkspace(workspace)
   152→  }
   153→
   154→  // Settings Management
   155→
   156→  getSettings(): Promise<Settings> {
   157→    return this.workspaceApi.getSettings()
   158→  }
   159→
   160→  saveSettings(settings: Settings): Promise<void> {
   161→    return this.workspaceApi.saveSettings(settings)
   162→  }
   163→
   164→  // Project Management
   165→
   166→  addProject(): Promise<string | null> {
   167→    return this.workspaceApi.addProject()
   168→  }
   169→
   170→  addProjectsFromParent(): Promise<Array<{ path: string; name: string }> | null> {
   171→    return this.workspaceApi.addProjectsFromParent()
   172→  }
   173→
   174→  // TTS
   175→
   176→  ttsInstallInstructions(projectPath: string): Promise<{ success: boolean }> {
   177→    return this.workspaceApi.ttsInstallInstructions(projectPath)
   178→  }
   179→
   180→  ttsSpeak(text: string): Promise<{ success: boolean; audioData?: string; error?: string }> {
   181→    return this.workspaceApi.ttsSpeak(text)
   182→  }
   183→
   184→  ttsStop(): Promise<{ success: boolean }> {
   185→    return this.workspaceApi.ttsStop()
   186→  }
   187→
   188→  // Events
   189→
   190→  onApiOpenSession(callback: ApiOpenSessionCallback): Unsubscribe {
   191→    return this.workspaceApi.onApiOpenSession(callback)
   192→  }
   193→
   194→  // Connection Management
   195→
   196→  getConnectionInfo(): { host: string; port: number; token: string } {
   197→    return {
   198→      host: this.host,
   199→      port: this.port,
   200→      token: this.connection.getToken()
   201→    }
   202→  }
   203→
   204→  async testConnection(): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }> {
   205→    try {
   206→      this.connection.setConnectionState('connecting')
   207→      const response = await fetch(`${this.connection.getBaseUrl()}/health`)
   208→
   209→      if (!response.ok) {
   210→        this.connection.setConnectionState('error', `HTTP ${response.status}`)
   211→        return { success: false, error: `HTTP ${response.status}` }
   212→      }
   213→
   214→      await response.json()
   215→      this.connection.setConnectionState('connected')
   216→      return { success: true }
   217→    } catch (error) {
   218→      const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Connection failed'
   219→      this.connection.setConnectionState('error', message)
   220→      return { success: false, error: message }
   221→    }
   222→  }
   223→
   224→  disconnect(): void {
   225→    this.wsManager.disconnectAll()
   226→    this.connection.setConnectionState('disconnected')
   227→  }
   228→}
   229→

```
1→/**
```
     2→ * Nonce Management Module
     3→ *
     4→ * Manages one-time handshake nonces for QR code security.
     5→ */
     6→
     7→import { randomBytes } from 'crypto'
     8→import type { NonceEntry } from './types.js'
     9→
    10→const NONCE_EXPIRY_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000 // 5 minutes
    11→const NONCE_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MS = 60 * 1000 // 1 minute
    12→
    13→const nonceStore = new Map<string, NonceEntry>()
    14→let cleanupInterval: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null = null
    15→
    16→/**
    17→ * Start the nonce cleanup interval
    18→ */
    19→export function startNonceCleanup(): void {
    20→  if (cleanupInterval) return
    21→
    22→  cleanupInterval = setInterval(() => {
    23→    const now = Date.now()
    24→    for (const [nonce, entry] of nonceStore.entries()) {
    25→      if (entry.expiresAt < now || entry.used) {
    26→        nonceStore.delete(nonce)
    27→      }
    28→    }
    29→  }, NONCE_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MS)
    30→}
    31→
    32→/**
    33→ * Stop the nonce cleanup interval
    34→ */
    35→export function stopNonceCleanup(): void {
    36→  if (cleanupInterval) {
    37→    clearInterval(cleanupInterval)
    38→    cleanupInterval = null
    39→  }
    40→}
    41→
    42→/**
    43→ * Create a new one-time nonce for QR code handshake
    44→ */
    45→export function createNonce(): { nonce: string; expiresAt: number } {
    46→  const nonce = randomBytes(16).toString('hex')
    47→  const now = Date.now()
    48→  const expiresAt = now + NONCE_EXPIRY_MS
    49→
    50→  nonceStore.set(nonce, {
    51→    nonce,
    52→    createdAt: now,
    53→    expiresAt,
    54→    used: false
    55→  })
    56→
    57→  return { nonce, expiresAt }
    58→}
    59→
    60→/**
    61→ * Verify and consume a nonce
    62→ * Returns true if nonce is valid and not yet used
    63→ */
    64→export function verifyNonce(nonce: string): boolean {
    65→  const entry = nonceStore.get(nonce)
    66→
    67→  if (!entry) {
    68→    return false
    69→  }
    70→
    71→  const now = Date.now()
    72→
    73→  // Check if expired
    74→  if (entry.expiresAt < now) {
    75→    nonceStore.delete(nonce)
    76→    return false
    77→  }
    78→
    79→  // Check if already used
    80→  if (entry.used) {
    81→    return false
    82→  }
    83→
    84→  // Mark as used
    85→  entry.used = true
    86→
    87→  return true
    88→}
    89→
    90→/**
    91→ * Get nonce info without consuming it (for debugging)
    92→ */
    93→export function getNonceInfo(nonce: string): NonceEntry | null {
    94→  return nonceStore.get(nonce) || null
    95→}
    96→
    97→export const nonceConfig = {
    98→  NONCE_EXPIRY_MS,
    99→  NONCE_CLEANUP_INTERVAL_MS
   100→} as const
   101→

content.text:
Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile the detailed report:

## Comprehensive Connectivity Code Analysis Report

I've thoroughly explored the Claude Terminal codebase and identified all key connectivity-related code. Here's what I found:

### 1. **Orchestrator Port Fallback**
**File:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/orchestrator-api.ts`

- **Port Configuration:** Fixed to `ORCHESTRATOR_PORT = 19836`
- **Fallback Logic:** Lines 53-60
  - On `EADDRINUSE` error, attempts to bind to `ORCHESTRATOR_PORT + 1`
  - Simple one-time fallback (no retry loop, just one alternative port)
  - Only binds to localhost (`127.0.0.1`)

**Issue Found:** The fallback doesn't recursively try to increment ports; it only tries the next port once with `.listen()`.

---

### 2. **Rate Limiter Implementation**
**Files:**
- `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/mobile-security/auth-rate-limit.ts`
- `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/mobile-security/endpoint-rate-limit.ts`
- `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/api-server.ts`

**Auth Rate Limiting (auth-rate-limit.ts):**
- Max attempts: 5
- Window: 15 minutes (`RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS = 15 * 60 * 1000`)
- Block duration: 15 minutes (`RATE_LIMIT_BLOCK_DURATION_MS = 15 * 60 * 1000`)
- Window reset logic: Lines 36-40, 62-70
  - Checks if `now - entry.lastAttempt > RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS`
  - Resets entry if window expired before incrementing attempts

**Endpoint Rate Limiting (endpoint-rate-limit.ts):**
- Per-endpoint limits defined in `ENDPOINT_RATE_LIMITS` object
- Window: 1 minute (`ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS = 60 * 1000`)
- Cleanup function: Lines 97-104 (cleans up entries 2x window size old)
- Window reset: Lines 73-77 (resets when `now - entry.windowStart >= ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS`)

**API Server Rate Limiter (api-server.ts):**
- Max requests: 10 per minute
- Window: 1 minute (`RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS = 60000`)
- Cleanup: Random 10% chance on each request (line 109)
- Window reset: Lines 52-54 (same pattern as others)

---

### 3. **API Prompt Delay (4-Second Race Condition)**
**Files:**
- `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/app/api-prompt-handler.ts`
- `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/app/ipc-handlers/pty.ts`

**The Issue:** Lines 82-85 in pty.ts:
```typescript
setTimeout(() => {
  ptyManager.write(id, pending.prompt + '\n')
  pending.resolve({ success: true, message: 'Prompt sent to new terminal', sessionCreated: true })
}, 4000)  // ← 4-second hardcoded delay!
```

**Why This Exists:**
- Waits for new PTY to fully initialize and be ready
- But this is a **race condition** - if the PTY takes longer than 4 seconds to start, the prompt may be sent before it's ready
- No verification that the PTY is actually ready

**Related Timeout:** Line 72-78 in api-prompt-handler.ts:
- `API_SESSION_TIMEOUT_MS = 30000` (defined in `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/constants.ts`)

---

### 4. **Pending Prompt on Spawn Failure**
**File:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/app/ipc-handlers/pty.ts`

**Issue Pattern:** Lines 44-86
- `pending` prompts are stored in `pendingApiPrompts` Map (from `api-prompt-handler.ts`)
- When spawn succeeds, the pending prompt is sent after 4 seconds
- **Problem:** If spawn fails (throws error at line 91), the pending prompt is never resolved
- The timeout in `api-prompt-handler.ts` (line 72-78) eventually times out after 30 seconds with an error

**No Graceful Handling:** There's no try-catch around the spawn to resolve pending prompts with an error before throwing.

---

### 5. **Mobile Token Regeneration**
**File:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/mobile-server/token-manager.ts`

**Token Handling:**
- `loadOrCreateToken()` - Lines 20-56
  - Loads encrypted token from disk or generates new one
  - Fallback migration path for old unencrypted tokens (lines 34-39)
  - Uses `encryptToken()` for storage

- `regenerateToken()` - Lines 68-78

     7→function maybeRespondToCursorPositionRequest(
     8→  ptyManager: PtyManager,
     9→  ptyToBackend: Map<string, string>,
    10→  ptyId: string,
    11→  data: string
    12→): void {
    13→  const backend = ptyToBackend.get(ptyId)
    14→  if (!backend || backend === 'claude') return
    15→  if (data.includes('\x1b[6n') || data.includes('\x1b[?6n')) {
    16→    ptyManager.write(ptyId, '\x1b[1;1R')
    17→  }
    18→}

    94→
    95→  ipcMain.on('pty:write', (_, { id, data }: { id: string; data: string }) => ptyManager.write(id, data))
    96→  ipcMain.on('pty:resize', (_, { id, cols, rows }: { id: string; cols: number; rows: number }) => ptyManager.resize(id, cols, rows))

1→/**
     2→ * WebSocket Manager - WebSocket handling
     3→ */
     4→
     5→import { WebSocket, WebSocketServer } from 'ws'
     6→import { Server } from 'http'
     7→import { log } from './utils'
     8→import { PendingFile } from './types'
     9→
    10→export interface WebSocketManagerDeps {
    11→  getToken: () => string
    12→  getPtyManager: () => any
    13→  getPort: () => number
    14→  getTerminalSubscriptions: () => Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
    15→  getPtyStreams: () => Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
    16→  getPtyDataBuffer: () => Map<string, string[]>
    17→  getConnectedClients: () => Set<WebSocket>
    18→  getPendingFiles: () => Map<string, PendingFile>
    19→}
    20→
    21→export function setupWebSocket(server: Server, deps: WebSocketManagerDeps): WebSocketServer {
    22→  const wss = new WebSocketServer({ noServer: true })
    23→
    24→  // Handle upgrade requests manually to support multiple paths
    25→  server.on('upgrade', (req, socket, head) => {
    26→    const url = new URL(req.url || '', `http://localhost:${deps.getPort()}`)
    27→    const pathname = url.pathname
    28→
    29→    // Get token from Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header (preferred) or query string (fallback)
    30→    const protocolHeader = req.headers['sec-websocket-protocol'] as string | undefined
    31→    const protocols = protocolHeader?.split(',').map(p => p.trim()) || []
    32→    const tokenFromProtocol = protocols.find(p => p.startsWith('token-'))?.slice(6)
    33→    const tokenFromQuery = url.searchParams.get('token')
    34→    const token = tokenFromProtocol || tokenFromQuery
    35→
    36→    log('WebSocket upgrade request', {
    37→      url: req.url,
    38→      pathname,
    39→      hasProtocolToken: !!tokenFromProtocol,
    40→      hasQueryToken: !!tokenFromQuery,
    41→      headers: {
    42→        host: req.headers.host,
    43→        origin: req.headers.origin,
    44→        upgrade: req.headers.upgrade
    45→      }
    46→    })
    47→
    48→    // Validate token for all WebSocket connections
    49→    if (token !== deps.getToken()) {
    50→      log('WebSocket auth failed', { providedToken: token?.slice(0, 8) })
    51→      socket.write('HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized\r\n\r\n')
    52→      socket.destroy()
    53→      return
    54→    }
    55→
    56→    // Handle PTY stream WebSocket: /api/pty/:id/stream
    57→    const ptyStreamMatch = pathname.match(/^\/api\/pty\/([^/]+)\/stream$/)
    58→    if (ptyStreamMatch) {
    59→      const ptyId = ptyStreamMatch[1]
    60→      handlePtyStreamUpgrade(req, socket, head, ptyId, deps)
    61→      return
    62→    }
    63→
    64→    // Handle main WebSocket path: /ws
    65→    if (pathname === '/ws') {
    66→      wss.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head, (ws) => {
    67→        wss.emit('connection', ws, req)
    68→      })
    69→      return
    70→    }
    71→
    72→    // Unknown WebSocket path
    73→    log('Unknown WebSocket path', { pathname })
    74→    socket.write('HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found\r\n\r\n')
    75→    socket.destroy()
    76→  })
    77→
    78→  wss.on('connection', (ws: WebSocket, _req) => {
    79→    log('WebSocket client connected (main)')
    80→
    81→    deps.getConnectedClients().add(ws)
    82→
    83→    ws.on('message', (message: Buffer) => {
    84→      try {
    85→        const msg = JSON.parse(message.toString())
    86→        handleWebSocketMessage(ws, msg, deps)
    87→      } catch (e) {
    88→        log('Invalid WebSocket message', { error: String(e) })
    89→      }
    90→    })
    91→
    92→    ws.on('close', () => {
    93→      deps.getConnectedClients().delete(ws)
    94→
    95→      deps.getTerminalSubscriptions().forEach((subscribers, ptyId) => {
    96→        subscribers.delete(ws)
    97→        if (subscribers.size === 0) {
    98→          deps.getTerminalSubscriptions().delete(ptyId)
    99→        }
   100→      })
   101→      log('WebSocket client disconnected (main)')
   102→    })
   103→
   104→    // Send welcome message
   105→    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'connected', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   106→
   107→    // Send any pending files to new client
   108→    sendPendingFilesToClient(ws, deps.getPendingFiles())
   109→  })
   110→
   111→  return wss
   112→}
   113→
   114→function handlePtyStreamUpgrade(
   115→  req: any,
   116→  socket: any,
   117→  head: any,
   118→  ptyId: string,
   119→  deps: WebSocketManagerDeps
   120→): void {
   121→  const ptyManager = deps.getPtyManager()
   122→  if (!ptyManager || !ptyManager.getProcess(ptyId)) {
   123→    log('PTY stream upgrade failed - PTY not found', { ptyId })
   124→    socket.write('HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found\r\n\r\n')
   125→    socket.destroy()
   126→    return
   127→  }
   128→
   129→  const streamWss = new WebSocketServer({ noServer: true })
   130→
   131→  streamWss.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head, (ws) => {
   132→    log('PTY stream connected', { ptyId })
   133→
   134→    const ptyStreams = deps.getPtyStreams()
   135→    if (!ptyStreams.has(ptyId)) {
   136→      ptyStreams.set(ptyId, new Set())
   137→    }
   138→    ptyStreams.get(ptyId)!.add(ws)
   139→
   140→    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'connected', ptyId }))
   141→
   142→    // Flush any buffered data
   143→    flushPtyBuffer(ptyId, ws, deps.getPtyDataBuffer())
   144→
   145→    ws.on('message', (message: Buffer) => {
   146→      try {
   147→        const msg = JSON.parse(message.toString())
   148→
   149→        switch (msg.type) {
   150→          case 'input':
   151→            if (msg.data && ptyManager) {
   152→              ptyManager.write(ptyId, msg.data)
   153→            }
   154→            break
   155→
   156→          case 'resize':
   157→            if (msg.cols && msg.rows && ptyManager) {
   158→              ptyManager.resize(ptyId, msg.cols, msg.rows)
   159→            }
   160→            break
   161→
   162→          case 'ping':
   163→            ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'pong', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   164→            break
   165→
   166→          default:
   167→            log('Unknown PTY stream message type', { type: msg.type, ptyId })
   168→        }
   169→      } catch (e) {
   170→        log('Invalid PTY stream message', { error: String(e), ptyId })
   171→      }
   172→    })
   173→
   174→    ws.on('close', () => {
   175→      log('PTY stream disconnected', { ptyId })
   176→      ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   177→      if (ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.size === 0) {
   178→        ptyStreams.delete(ptyId)
   179→      }
   180→    })
   181→
   182→    ws.on('error', (err) => {
   183→      log('PTY stream error', { error: String(err), ptyId })
   184→      ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   185→    })
   186→  })
   187→}
   188→
   189→function handleWebSocketMessage(ws: WebSocket, msg: any, deps: WebSocketManagerDeps): void {
   190→  const ptyManager = deps.getPtyManager()
   191→  const terminalSubscriptions = deps.getTerminalSubscriptions()
   192→
   193→  switch (msg.type) {
   194→    case 'subscribe':
   195→      if (msg.ptyId) {
   196→        if (!terminalSubscriptions.has(msg.ptyId)) {
   197→          terminalSubscriptions.set(msg.ptyId, new Set())
   198→        }
   199→        terminalSubscriptions.get(msg.ptyId)!.add(ws)
   200→        ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'subscribed', ptyId: msg.ptyId }))
   201→      }
   202→      break
   203→
   204→    case 'unsubscribe':
   205→      if (msg.ptyId) {
   206→        terminalSubscriptions.get(msg.ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   207→      }
   208→      break
   209→
   210→    case 'write':
   211→      if (msg.ptyId && msg.data && ptyManager) {
   212→        ptyManager.write(msg.ptyId, msg.data)
   213→      }
   214→      break
   215→
   216→    case 'resize':
   217→      if (msg.ptyId && msg.cols && msg.rows && ptyManager) {
   218→        ptyManager.resize(msg.ptyId, msg.cols, msg.rows)
   219→      }
   220→      break
   221→
   222→    case 'ping':
   223→      ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'pong', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   224→      break
   225→  }
   226→}
   227→
   228→function flushPtyBuffer(ptyId: string, ws: WebSocket, ptyDataBuffer: Map<string, string[]>): void {
   229→  const buffer = ptyDataBuffer.get(ptyId)
   230→  if (!buffer || buffer.length === 0) return
   231→
   232→  buffer.forEach(data => {
   233→    if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   234→      ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'data', data }))
   235→    }
   236→  })
   237→
   238→  ptyDataBuffer.delete(ptyId)
   239→}
   240→
   241→function sendPendingFilesToClient(ws: WebSocket, pendingFiles: Map<string, PendingFile>): void {
   242→  const now = Date.now()
   243→  // Clean up expired files first
   244→  for (const [fileId, file] of pendingFiles) {

   255→    message: f.message

```
     1→/**
     2→ * WebSocket Manager - WebSocket handling
     3→ */
     4→
     5→import { WebSocket, WebSocketServer } from 'ws'
     6→import { Server } from 'http'
     7→import { log } from './utils'
     8→import { PendingFile } from './types'
     9→
    10→export interface WebSocketManagerDeps {
    11→  getToken: () => string
    12→  getPtyManager: () => any
    13→  getPort: () => number
    14→  getTerminalSubscriptions: () => Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
    15→  getPtyStreams: () => Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
    16→  getPtyDataBuffer: () => Map<string, string[]>
    17→  getConnectedClients: () => Set<WebSocket>
    18→  getPendingFiles: () => Map<string, PendingFile>
    19→}
    20→
    21→export function setupWebSocket(server: Server, deps: WebSocketManagerDeps): WebSocketServer {
    22→  const wss = new WebSocketServer({ noServer: true })
    23→
    24→  // Handle upgrade requests manually to support multiple paths
    25→  server.on('upgrade', (req, socket, head) => {
    26→    const url = new URL(req.url || '', `http://localhost:${deps.getPort()}`)
    27→    const pathname = url.pathname
    28→
    29→    // Get token from Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header (preferred) or query string (fallback)
    30→    const protocolHeader = req.headers['sec-websocket-protocol'] as string | undefined
    31→    const protocols = protocolHeader?.split(',').map(p => p.trim()) || []
    32→    const tokenFromProtocol = protocols.find(p => p.startsWith('token-'))?.slice(6)
    33→    const tokenFromQuery = url.searchParams.get('token')
    34→    const token = tokenFromProtocol || tokenFromQuery
    35→
    36→    log('WebSocket upgrade request', {
    37→      url: req.url,
    38→      pathname,
    39→      hasProtocolToken: !!tokenFromProtocol,
    40→      hasQueryToken: !!tokenFromQuery,
    41→      headers: {
    42→        host: req.headers.host,
    43→        origin: req.headers.origin,
    44→        upgrade: req.headers.upgrade
    45→      }
    46→    })
    47→
    48→    // Validate token for all WebSocket connections
    49→    if (token !== deps.getToken()) {
    50→      log('WebSocket auth failed', { providedToken: token?.slice(0, 8) })
    51→      socket.write('HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized\r\n\r\n')
    52→      socket.destroy()
    53→      return
    54→    }
    55→
    56→    // Handle PTY stream WebSocket: /api/pty/:id/stream
    57→    const ptyStreamMatch = pathname.match(/^\/api\/pty\/([^/]+)\/stream$/)
    58→    if (ptyStreamMatch) {
    59→      const ptyId = ptyStreamMatch[1]
    60→      handlePtyStreamUpgrade(req, socket, head, ptyId, deps)
    61→      return
    62→    }
    63→
    64→    // Handle main WebSocket path: /ws
    65→    if (pathname === '/ws') {
    66→      wss.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head, (ws) => {
    67→        wss.emit('connection', ws, req)
    68→      })
    69→      return
    70→    }
    71→
    72→    // Unknown WebSocket path
    73→    log('Unknown WebSocket path', { pathname })
    74→    socket.write('HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found\r\n\r\n')
    75→    socket.destroy()
    76→  })
    77→
    78→  wss.on('connection', (ws: WebSocket, _req) => {
    79→    log('WebSocket client connected (main)')
    80→
    81→    deps.getConnectedClients().add(ws)
    82→
    83→    ws.on('message', (message: Buffer) => {
    84→      try {
    85→        const msg = JSON.parse(message.toString())
    86→        handleWebSocketMessage(ws, msg, deps)
    87→      } catch (e) {
    88→        log('Invalid WebSocket message', { error: String(e) })
    89→      }
    90→    })
    91→
    92→    ws.on('close', () => {
    93→      deps.getConnectedClients().delete(ws)
    94→
    95→      deps.getTerminalSubscriptions().forEach((subscribers, ptyId) => {
    96→        subscribers.delete(ws)
    97→        if (subscribers.size === 0) {
    98→          deps.getTerminalSubscriptions().delete(ptyId)
    99→        }
   100→      })
   101→      log('WebSocket client disconnected (main)')
   102→    })
   103→
   104→    // Send welcome message
   105→    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'connected', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   106→
   107→    // Send any pending files to new client
   108→    sendPendingFilesToClient(ws, deps.getPendingFiles())
   109→  })
   110→
   111→  return wss
   112→}
   113→
   114→function handlePtyStreamUpgrade(
   115→  req: any,
   116→  socket: any,
   117→  head: any,
   118→  ptyId: string,
   119→  deps: WebSocketManagerDeps
   120→): void {
   121→  const ptyManager = deps.getPtyManager()
   122→  if (!ptyManager || !ptyManager.getProcess(ptyId)) {
   123→    log('PTY stream upgrade failed - PTY not found', { ptyId })
   124→    socket.write('HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found\r\n\r\n')
   125→    socket.destroy()
   126→    return
   127→  }
   128→
   129→  const streamWss = new WebSocketServer({ noServer: true })
   130→
   131→  streamWss.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head, (ws) => {
   132→    log('PTY stream connected', { ptyId })
   133→
   134→    const ptyStreams = deps.getPtyStreams()
   135→    if (!ptyStreams.has(ptyId)) {
   136→      ptyStreams.set(ptyId, new Set())
   137→    }
   138→    ptyStreams.get(ptyId)!.add(ws)
   139→
   140→    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'connected', ptyId }))
   141→
   142→    // Flush any buffered data
   143→    flushPtyBuffer(ptyId, ws, deps.getPtyDataBuffer())
   144→
   145→    ws.on('message', (message: Buffer) => {
   146→      try {
   147→        const msg = JSON.parse(message.toString())
   148→
   149→        switch (msg.type) {
   150→          case 'input':
   151→            if (msg.data && ptyManager) {
   152→              ptyManager.write(ptyId, msg.data)
   153→            }
   154→            break
   155→
   156→          case 'resize':
   157→            if (msg.cols && msg.rows && ptyManager) {
   158→              ptyManager.resize(ptyId, msg.cols, msg.rows)
   159→            }
   160→            break
   161→
   162→          case 'ping':
   163→            ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'pong', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   164→            break
   165→
   166→          default:
   167→            log('Unknown PTY stream message type', { type: msg.type, ptyId })
   168→        }
   169→      } catch (e) {
   170→        log('Invalid PTY stream message', { error: String(e), ptyId })
   171→      }
   172→    })
   173→
   174→    ws.on('close', () => {
   175→      log('PTY stream disconnected', { ptyId })
   176→      ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   177→      if (ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.size === 0) {
   178→        ptyStreams.delete(ptyId)
   179→      }
   180→    })
   181→
   182→    ws.on('error', (err) => {
   183→      log('PTY stream error', { error: String(err), ptyId })
   184→      ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   185→    })
   186→  })
   187→}
   188→
   189→function handleWebSocketMessage(ws: WebSocket, msg: any, deps: WebSocketManagerDeps): void {
   190→  const ptyManager = deps.getPtyManager()
   191→  const terminalSubscriptions = deps.getTerminalSubscriptions()
   192→
   193→  switch (msg.type) {
   194→    case 'subscribe':
   195→      if (msg.ptyId) {
   196→        if (!terminalSubscriptions.has(msg.ptyId)) {
   197→          terminalSubscriptions.set(msg.ptyId, new Set())
   198→        }
   199→        terminalSubscriptions.get(msg.ptyId)!.add(ws)
   200→        ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'subscribed', ptyId: msg.ptyId }))
   201→      }
   202→      break
   203→
   204→    case 'unsubscribe':
   205→      if (msg.ptyId) {
   206→        terminalSubscriptions.get(msg.ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   207→      }
   208→      break
   209→
   210→    case 'write':
   211→      if (msg.ptyId && msg.data && ptyManager) {
   212→        ptyManager.write(msg.ptyId, msg.data)
   213→      }
   214→      break
   215→
   216→    case 'resize':
   217→      if (msg.ptyId && msg.cols && msg.rows && ptyManager) {
   218→        ptyManager.resize(msg.ptyId, msg.cols, msg.rows)
   219→      }
   220→      break
   221→
   222→    case 'ping':
   223→      ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'pong', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   224→      break
   225→  }
   226→}
   227→
   228→function flushPtyBuffer(ptyId: string, ws: WebSocket, ptyDataBuffer: Map<string, string[]>): void {
   229→  const buffer = ptyDataBuffer.get(ptyId)
   230→  if (!buffer || buffer.length === 0) return
   231→
   232→  buffer.forEach(data => {
   233→    if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   234→      ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'data', data }))
   235→    }
   236→  })
   237→
   238→  ptyDataBuffer.delete(ptyId)
   239→}
   240→
   241→function sendPendingFilesToClient(ws: WebSocket, pendingFiles: Map<string, PendingFile>): void {
   242→  const now = Date.now()
   243→  // Clean up expired files first
   244→  for (const [fileId, file] of pendingFiles) {
   245→    if (file.expiresAt < now) {
   246→      pendingFiles.delete(fileId)
   247→    }
   248→  }
   249→
   250→  const files = Array.from(pendingFiles.values()).map(f => ({
   251→    id: f.id,
   252→    name: f.name,
   253→    size: f.size,
   254→    mimeType: f.mimeType,
   255→    message: f.message
   256→  }))
   257→
   258→  if (files.length > 0) {
   259→    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'files:pending', files }))
   260→  }
   261→}
   262→
   263→export function broadcastTerminalData(
   264→  ptyId: string,
   265→  data: string,
   266→  terminalSubscriptions: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
   267→): void {
   268→  const subscribers = terminalSubscriptions.get(ptyId)
   269→  if (!subscribers) return
   270→
   271→  const message = JSON.stringify({
   272→    type: 'terminal-data',
   273→    ptyId,
   274→    data
   275→  })
   276→
   277→  subscribers.forEach(ws => {
   278→    if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   279→      ws.send(message)
   280→    }
   281→  })
   282→}
   283→
   284→export function broadcastPtyData(
   285→  ptyId: string,
   286→  data: string,
   287→  ptyStreams: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>,
   288→  ptyDataBuffer: Map<string, string[]>
   289→): void {
   290→  const streams = ptyStreams.get(ptyId)
   291→
   292→  // If no streams connected, buffer the data
   293→  if (!streams || streams.size === 0) {
   294→    if (!ptyDataBuffer.has(ptyId)) {
   295→      ptyDataBuffer.set(ptyId, [])
   296→    }
   297→    const buffer = ptyDataBuffer.get(ptyId)!
   298→    buffer.push(data)
   299→    // Limit buffer size
   300→    if (buffer.length > 1000) {
   301→      buffer.shift()
   302→    }
   303→    return
   304→  }
   305→
   306→  const message = JSON.stringify({
   307→    type: 'data',
   308→    data

   311→  streams.forEach(ws => {
   312→    if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   313→      ws.send(message)

   317→
```

1→/**
     2→ * Authentication Rate Limiting Module
     3→ *
     4→ * Provides rate limiting for failed authentication attempts.
     5→ */
     6→
     7→import type { RateLimitEntry } from './types.js'
     8→
     9→// Constants
    10→const RATE_LIMIT_MAX_ATTEMPTS = 5
    11→const RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS = 15 * 60 * 1000 // 15 minutes
    12→const RATE_LIMIT_BLOCK_DURATION_MS = 15 * 60 * 1000 // 15 minutes
    13→
    14→const rateLimitStore = new Map<string, RateLimitEntry>()
    15→
    16→/**
    17→ * Check if an IP is rate limited
    18→ * Returns { allowed: boolean, retryAfter?: number }
    19→ */
    20→export function checkRateLimit(ip: string): { allowed: boolean; retryAfter?: number } {
    21→  const now = Date.now()
    22→  const entry = rateLimitStore.get(ip)
    23→
    24→  if (!entry) {
    25→    return { allowed: true }
    26→  }
    27→
    28→  // Check if currently blocked
    29→  if (entry.blockedUntil && entry.blockedUntil > now) {
    30→    return {
    31→      allowed: false,
    32→      retryAfter: Math.ceil((entry.blockedUntil - now) / 1000)
    33→    }
    34→  }
    35→
    36→  // Check if window has expired - reset if so
    37→  if (now - entry.lastAttempt > RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS) {
    38→    rateLimitStore.delete(ip)
    39→    return { allowed: true }
    40→  }
    41→
    42→  return { allowed: true }
    43→}
    44→
    45→/**
    46→ * Record a failed authentication attempt for an IP
    47→ * Returns true if the IP is now blocked
    48→ */
    49→export function recordFailedAuth(ip: string): boolean {
    50→  const now = Date.now()
    51→  const entry = rateLimitStore.get(ip)
    52→
    53→  if (!entry) {
    54→    rateLimitStore.set(ip, {
    55→      attempts: 1,
    56→      lastAttempt: now,
    57→      blockedUntil: null
    58→    })
    59→    return false
    60→  }
    61→
    62→  // Reset if window has expired
    63→  if (now - entry.lastAttempt > RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS) {
    64→    rateLimitStore.set(ip, {
    65→      attempts: 1,
    66→      lastAttempt: now,
    67→      blockedUntil: null
    68→    })
    69→    return false
    70→  }
    71→
    72→  // Increment attempts
    73→  entry.attempts++
    74→  entry.lastAttempt = now
    75→
    76→  // Block if too many attempts
    77→  if (entry.attempts >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_ATTEMPTS) {
    78→    entry.blockedUntil = now + RATE_LIMIT_BLOCK_DURATION_MS
    79→    return true
    80→  }
    81→
    82→  return false
    83→}
    84→
    85→/**
    86→ * Clear rate limit for an IP (call on successful auth)
    87→ */
    88→export function clearRateLimit(ip: string): void {
    89→  rateLimitStore.delete(ip)
    90→}
    91→
    92→/**
    93→ * Get rate limit status for debugging
    94→ */
    95→export function getRateLimitStatus(ip: string): RateLimitEntry | null {
    96→  return rateLimitStore.get(ip) || null
    97→}
    98→
    99→export const authRateLimitConfig = {
   100→  RATE_LIMIT_MAX_ATTEMPTS,
   101→  RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS,
   102→  RATE_LIMIT_BLOCK_DURATION_MS
   103→} as const
   104→

     6→
     7→import type { EndpointRateLimitEntry } from './types.js'
     8→
     9→// Store: ip:endpoint -> { count, windowStart }
    10→const endpointRateLimitStore = new Map<string, EndpointRateLimitEntry>()
    11→
    12→// Default limits per endpoint category (requests per minute)
    13→const ENDPOINT_RATE_LIMITS: Record<string, number> = {

    22→  'DELETE:/projects/beads': 20,
    23→  // Read operations have higher limits
    24→  'GET:/api': 60,
    25→  'GET:/projects': 60,
    26→  // Default fallback
    27→  default: 60
    28→}
    29→
    30→const ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS = 60 * 1000 // 1 minute window
    31→
    32→/**
    33→ * Get the rate limit for an endpoint
    34→ */
    35→function getEndpointRateLimit(method: string, path: string): number {
    36→  // Try exact match first
    37→  const exactKey = `${method}:${path}`
    38→  if (ENDPOINT_RATE_LIMITS[exactKey]) {
    39→    return ENDPOINT_RATE_LIMITS[exactKey]
    40→  }
    41→
    42→  // Try prefix matches
    43→  for (const [key, limit] of Object.entries(ENDPOINT_RATE_LIMITS)) {
    44→    if (exactKey.startsWith(key)) {
    45→      return limit
    46→    }
    47→  }
    48→
    49→  return ENDPOINT_RATE_LIMITS['default']
    50→}
    51→
    52→/**
    53→ * Check if a request is rate limited by endpoint
    54→ * Returns { allowed: boolean, remaining: number, resetIn: number }
    55→ */
    56→export function checkEndpointRateLimit(
    57→  ip: string,
    58→  method: string,
    59→  path: string
    60→): { allowed: boolean; remaining: number; resetIn: number } {
    61→  const now = Date.now()
    62→  const limit = getEndpointRateLimit(method, path)
    63→  const key = `${ip}:${method}:${path}`
    64→
    65→  const entry = endpointRateLimitStore.get(key)
    66→
    67→  if (!entry) {
    68→    // First request in this window
    69→    endpointRateLimitStore.set(key, { count: 1, windowStart: now })
    70→    return { allowed: true, remaining: limit - 1, resetIn: ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS }
    71→  }
    72→
    73→  // Check if window has expired
    74→  if (now - entry.windowStart >= ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS) {
    75→    // Start new window
    76→    endpointRateLimitStore.set(key, { count: 1, windowStart: now })
    77→    return { allowed: true, remaining: limit - 1, resetIn: ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS }
    78→  }
    79→
    80→  // Within window - check count
    81→  if (entry.count >= limit) {
    82→    const resetIn = ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS - (now - entry.windowStart)
    83→    return { allowed: false, remaining: 0, resetIn }
    84→  }
    85→
    86→  // Increment count
    87→  entry.count++
    88→  const remaining = limit - entry.count
    89→  const resetIn = ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS - (now - entry.windowStart)
    90→  return { allowed: true, remaining, resetIn }
    91→}
    92→
    93→/**
    94→ * Clean up expired endpoint rate limit entries
    95→ * Call periodically to prevent memory growth
    96→ */
    97→export function cleanupEndpointRateLimits(): void {
    98→  const now = Date.now()
    99→  for (const [key, entry] of endpointRateLimitStore.entries()) {
   100→    if (now - entry.windowStart >= ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS * 2) {
   101→      endpointRateLimitStore.delete(key)
   102→    }
   103→  }
   104→}
   105→
   106→export const endpointRateLimitConfig = {
   107→  ENDPOINT_RATE_LIMITS,
   108→  ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS
   109→} as const
   110→

```
     1→/**
     2→ * Endpoint Rate Limiting Module
     3→ *
     4→ * Provides per-endpoint rate limiting for API requests.
     5→ */
     6→
     7→import type { EndpointRateLimitEntry } from './types.js'
     8→
     9→// Store: ip:endpoint -> { count, windowStart }
    10→const endpointRateLimitStore = new Map<string, EndpointRateLimitEntry>()
    11→
    12→// Default limits per endpoint category (requests per minute)
    13→const ENDPOINT_RATE_LIMITS: Record<string, number> = {
    14→  // Write operations are more restricted
    15→  'POST:/api/terminal/create': 10,
    16→  'POST:/api/pty/spawn': 10,
    17→  'POST:/api/pty/write': 120, // Higher limit for typing
    18→  'POST:/api/tts/speak': 30,
    19→  // Beads operations
    20→  'POST:/projects/beads': 30,
    21→  'PATCH:/projects/beads': 30,
    22→  'DELETE:/projects/beads': 20,
    23→  // Read operations have higher limits
    24→  'GET:/api': 60,
    25→  'GET:/projects': 60,
    26→  // Default fallback
    27→  default: 60
    28→}
    29→
    30→const ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS = 60 * 1000 // 1 minute window
    31→
    32→/**
    33→ * Get the rate limit for an endpoint
    34→ */
    35→function getEndpointRateLimit(method: string, path: string): number {
    36→  // Try exact match first
    37→  const exactKey = `${method}:${path}`
    38→  if (ENDPOINT_RATE_LIMITS[exactKey]) {
    39→    return ENDPOINT_RATE_LIMITS[exactKey]
    40→  }
    41→
    42→  // Try prefix matches
    43→  for (const [key, limit] of Object.entries(ENDPOINT_RATE_LIMITS)) {
    44→    if (exactKey.startsWith(key)) {
    45→      return limit
    46→    }
    47→  }
    48→
    49→  return ENDPOINT_RATE_LIMITS['default']
    50→}
    51→
    52→/**
    53→ * Check if a request is rate limited by endpoint
    54→ * Returns { allowed: boolean, remaining: number, resetIn: number }
    55→ */
    56→export function checkEndpointRateLimit(
    57→  ip: string,
    58→  method: string,
    59→  path: string
    60→): { allowed: boolean; remaining: number; resetIn: number } {
    61→  const now = Date.now()
    62→  const limit = getEndpointRateLimit(method, path)
    63→  const key = `${ip}:${method}:${path}`
    64→
    65→  const entry = endpointRateLimitStore.get(key)
    66→
    67→  if (!entry) {
    68→    // First request in this window
    69→    endpointRateLimitStore.set(key, { count: 1, windowStart: now })
    70→    return { allowed: true, remaining: limit - 1, resetIn: ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS }
    71→  }
    72→
    73→  // Check if window has expired
    74→  if (now - entry.windowStart >= ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS) {
    75→    // Start new window
    76→    endpointRateLimitStore.set(key, { count: 1, windowStart: now })
    77→    return { allowed: true, remaining: limit - 1, resetIn: ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS }
    78→  }
    79→
    80→  // Within window - check count
    81→  if (entry.count >= limit) {
    82→    const resetIn = ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS - (now - entry.windowStart)
    83→    return { allowed: false, remaining: 0, resetIn }
    84→  }
    85→
    86→  // Increment count
    87→  entry.count++
    88→  const remaining = limit - entry.count
    89→  const resetIn = ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS - (now - entry.windowStart)
    90→  return { allowed: true, remaining, resetIn }
    91→}
    92→
    93→/**
    94→ * Clean up expired endpoint rate limit entries
    95→ * Call periodically to prevent memory growth
    96→ */
    97→export function cleanupEndpointRateLimits(): void {
    98→  const now = Date.now()
    99→  for (const [key, entry] of endpointRateLimitStore.entries()) {
   100→    if (now - entry.windowStart >= ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS * 2) {
   101→      endpointRateLimitStore.delete(key)
   102→    }
   103→  }
   104→}
   105→
   106→export const endpointRateLimitConfig = {
   107→  ENDPOINT_RATE_LIMITS,
   108→  ENDPOINT_RATE_WINDOW_MS
   109→} as const
   110→
```

1→/**
     2→ * PTY WebSocket Management
     3→ *
     4→ * Manages WebSocket connections for PTY data streaming.
     5→ */
     6→
     7→import { MAX_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS, RECONNECT_DELAYS } from './constants'
     8→import { PtyWebSocketState } from './types'
     9→
    10→export class PtyWebSocketManager {
    11→  private ptyWebsockets: Map<string, PtyWebSocketState> = new Map()
    12→  private wsBaseUrl: string
    13→  private token: string
    14→
    15→  constructor(wsBaseUrl: string, token: string) {
    16→    this.wsBaseUrl = wsBaseUrl
    17→    this.token = token
    18→  }
    19→
    20→  getPtyWebsockets(): Map<string, PtyWebSocketState> {
    21→    return this.ptyWebsockets
    22→  }
    23→
    24→  /**
    25→   * Connect WebSocket for PTY data streaming
    26→   */
    27→  connectPtyStream(ptyId: string): void {
    28→    // Don't reconnect if already connected or connecting
    29→    const existing = this.ptyWebsockets.get(ptyId)
    30→    if (
    31→      existing?.ws &&
    32→      (existing.ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN || existing.ws.readyState === WebSocket.CONNECTING)
    33→    ) {
    34→      return
    35→    }
    36→
    37→    const url = `${this.wsBaseUrl}/api/pty/${ptyId}/stream?token=${encodeURIComponent(this.token)}`
    38→    console.log('[HttpBackend] Connecting PTY stream:', ptyId)
    39→
    40→    const ws = new WebSocket(url)
    41→
    42→    // Reuse existing state if available (preserves callbacks), otherwise create new
    43→    const state: PtyWebSocketState = existing || {
    44→      ws,
    45→      dataCallbacks: new Set(),
    46→      exitCallbacks: new Set(),
    47→      reconnectAttempts: 0,
    48→      reconnectTimer: null,
    49→      dataBuffer: []
    50→    }
    51→    // Update the WebSocket reference
    52→    state.ws = ws
    53→    state.reconnectAttempts = existing?.reconnectAttempts || 0
    54→
    55→    ws.onopen = () => {
    56→      console.log('[HttpBackend] PTY stream connected:', ptyId)
    57→      state.reconnectAttempts = 0
    58→    }
    59→
    60→    ws.onmessage = (event) => {
    61→      try {
    62→        const msg = JSON.parse(event.data)
    63→
    64→        switch (msg.type) {
    65→          case 'data':
    66→            // Buffer data if no callbacks registered yet
    67→            if (state.dataCallbacks.size === 0) {
    68→              state.dataBuffer.push(msg.data)
    69→              // Limit buffer size to prevent memory issues
    70→              if (state.dataBuffer.length > 1000) {
    71→                state.dataBuffer.shift()
    72→              }
    73→            } else {
    74→              // Forward data to all callbacks
    75→              state.dataCallbacks.forEach((cb) => cb(msg.data))
    76→            }
    77→            break
    78→
    79→          case 'exit':
    80→            // Forward exit to all callbacks
    81→            state.exitCallbacks.forEach((cb) => cb(msg.code))
    82→            // Clean up after exit
    83→            this.ptyWebsockets.delete(ptyId)
    84→            break
    85→
    86→          case 'connected':
    87→            console.log(
    88→              '[HttpBackend] PTY stream confirmed:',
    89→              ptyId,
    90→              'callbacks:',
    91→              state.dataCallbacks.size,
    92→              'buffered:',
    93→              state.dataBuffer.length
    94→            )
    95→            break
    96→
    97→          case 'pong':
    98→            // Keep-alive response, nothing to do
    99→            break
   100→
   101→          default:
   102→            console.log('[HttpBackend] Unknown PTY message type:', msg.type)
   103→        }
   104→      } catch (e) {
   105→        console.error('[HttpBackend] Failed to parse PTY message:', e)
   106→      }
   107→    }
   108→
   109→    ws.onerror = (event) => {
   110→      console.error('[HttpBackend] PTY stream error:', ptyId, event)
   111→    }
   112→
   113→    ws.onclose = (event) => {
   114→      console.log('[HttpBackend] PTY stream closed:', ptyId, event.code, event.reason)
   115→
   116→      // Attempt reconnection if not a clean close and we still want this PTY
   117→      if (!event.wasClean && state.reconnectAttempts < MAX_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS) {
   118→        const delay =
   119→          RECONNECT_DELAYS[Math.min(state.reconnectAttempts, RECONNECT_DELAYS.length - 1)]
   120→        console.log(`[HttpBackend] Reconnecting PTY stream in ${delay}ms...`)
   121→
   122→        state.reconnectTimer = setTimeout(() => {
   123→          state.reconnectAttempts++
   124→          this.connectPtyStream(ptyId)
   125→        }, delay)
   126→      } else if (event.code !== 1000) {
   127→        // Exit callback for abnormal closure
   128→        state.exitCallbacks.forEach((cb) => cb(-1))
   129→        this.ptyWebsockets.delete(ptyId)
   130→      }
   131→    }
   132→
   133→    this.ptyWebsockets.set(ptyId, state)
   134→  }
   135→
   136→  /**
   137→   * Disconnect PTY WebSocket stream
   138→   */
   139→  disconnectPtyStream(ptyId: string): void {
   140→    const state = this.ptyWebsockets.get(ptyId)
   141→    if (!state) return
   142→
   143→    // Clear reconnect timer
   144→    if (state.reconnectTimer) {
   145→      clearTimeout(state.reconnectTimer)
   146→    }
   147→
   148→    // Close WebSocket
   149→    if (
   150→      state.ws &&
   151→      (state.ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN || state.ws.readyState === WebSocket.CONNECTING)
   152→    ) {
   153→      state.ws.close(1000, 'Client requested close')
   154→    }
   155→
   156→    this.ptyWebsockets.delete(ptyId)
   157→  }
   158→
   159→  /**
   160→   * Disconnect all PTY WebSocket connections
   161→   */
   162→  disconnectAll(): void {
   163→    this.ptyWebsockets.forEach((state, ptyId) => {
   164→      if (state.reconnectTimer) {
   165→        clearTimeout(state.reconnectTimer)
   166→      }
   167→      if (
   168→        state.ws &&
   169→        (state.ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN || state.ws.readyState === WebSocket.CONNECTING)
   170→      ) {
   171→        state.ws.close(1000, 'Client disconnecting')
   172→      }
   173→    })
   174→    this.ptyWebsockets.clear()
   175→  }
   176→
   177→  /**
   178→   * Send data to a PTY via WebSocket
   179→   */
   180→  sendToPty(ptyId: string, message: object): boolean {
   181→    const state = this.ptyWebsockets.get(ptyId)
   182→    if (state && state.ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   183→      state.ws.send(JSON.stringify(message))
   184→      return true
   185→    }
   186→    return false
   187→  }
   188→}

```
   318→export function broadcastPtyExit(
   319→  ptyId: string,
   320→  code: number,
   321→  ptyStreams: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>,
   322→  terminalSubscriptions?: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
   323→): void {
   324→  const streams = ptyStreams.get(ptyId)
   325→  if (streams && streams.size > 0) {
   326→    const message = JSON.stringify({
   327→      type: 'exit',
   328→      code
   329→    })
   330→
   331→    streams.forEach(ws => {
   332→      if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   333→        ws.send(message)
   334→        ws.close(1000, 'PTY exited')
   335→      }
   336→    })
   337→  }
   338→
   339→  // Clean up stale subscriptions for this PTY
   340→  ptyStreams.delete(ptyId)
   341→  terminalSubscriptions?.delete(ptyId)
   342→}
   343→
```

rawInput.command:
cd "/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal" && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: connectivity bug audit — port fallback, rate limit, prompt race, CORS, WS cleanup

- Orchestrator port fallback: retry up to 5 ports instead of one-shot fallback
  that could loop or silently fail on second EADDRINUSE
- Auth rate limit: reset attempts when block period expires so users get fresh
  attempts instead of being immediately re-blocked on next failure
- API prompt 4s delay: verify PTY still exists before writing pending prompt,
  resolve with error if PTY exited during the delay window
- Pending prompt on spawn failure: resolve pending API prompt immediately with
  error instead of leaving it dangling for 30s timeout
- WebSocket subscription leak: clean up terminalSubscriptions and ptyStreams
  entries when PTY exits to prevent unbounded Map growth
- CORS origin validation: parse origin URL and match hostname exactly instead
  of substring matching (origin.includes('localhost') matched evil-localhost.com,
  origin.includes('.ts.net') matched evil.ts.net.attacker.com)

Task: @bug-connectivity
Spec: @orchestrator

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
